95924002.title: Malcolm's Nightmare...
summary: Malcolm watches in horror as Motoko mods his beloved Caliburn with chainguns, missiles, and armored plating. He wakes from a nightmare of Motoko approaching him with a toolbox and saw, only to find the real Motoko working on his car while Hiromi happily gets her bike upgraded.

95925616.title: Tinfoil protection
summary: Motoko tries and fails to get Jun to exercise, then hands him a tinfoil hat as his 'perfect firewall' when he begs for netrunner protection. Jun remains blissfully unaware that the hat is worthless.

95934662.title: Tin foil hat... Infinite protection
summary: Motoko slaps a tinfoil hat on Jun as a joke firewall, then notices she gained 10,000 Technical Ability XP. Jun rides NCART through Sixth Street turf and finds the hat actually works -- repelling picksockets and blocking malware while he pirates all ten Bushido movies. Hiromi calls to scream about the business opportunity after Fujimura offers 500 million eddies for a thousand units.

95934836.title: That's your imagination
summary: A salesman pitches tinfoil hat 'firewalls' in different corporate editions -- Manager, Executive -- while the buyer points out they look like Mickey Mouse ears and dunce caps.

95968886.title: Becca is sensitive, you know?
summary: Rebecca and Sasha go undercover in Japantown on a favor for Gloria Martinez, trying to dig up intel on Section 9 and what Gloria's kid David has gotten into. They chat up Jun at a bar but the Oni refuses to give up deets on his sister's crew. Becca keeps a hand on her Omaha as they leave Tyger Claw turf.

95975288.title: No mistake has ever happened copying Bushido 4. 5 on the other hand? Blegh.
summary: Hiromi presents a school project on demographic analysis, using hidden camera footage of a borg receiving a personalized bouquet of artisanal grenades -- frag, flashbang, EMP, incendiary, and tear gas -- each with custom engraved messages. The teacher sighs and heads off to talk to security again.

95981765.title: a pure angel that only serves truth and justice
summary: N54 News reports the unprecedented kidnapping of Saburo Arasaka while the city goes into lockdown. Jun confronts Motoko on the couch, and she admits her encrypted taunt to an Edgerunner crew -- telling them to ask Arasaka's CEO for deets on her -- may have been taken literally. Jun wonders why his sister can't just shoplift like a normal brat.

95982323.title: What netrunner!?
summary: Motoko tells Hiromi that a cute Korean netrunner asked her to shoot down Space Force One. Hiromi ignores the presidential assassination angle entirely and zeroes in on demanding to know who this netrunner bitch is.

95990975.title: Pre-teen Johnny
summary: An alternate take where V brings Johnny Silverhand to the Afterlife -- except the Relic put him in a pre-teen body. Rogue doesn't recognize the kid until he starts cursing in Johnny's voice. V rolls on the floor laughing while mini-Johnny tries to shoot her.

95996939.title: Out of Town, BRB
summary: Jun, Ichi, and Malcolm each discover Motoko left Night City for a transport gig to Seattle with a nomad friend. Jun panics but can't chase her across the Badlands on a bike. Ichi sighs at her loyalty. Malcolm barely notices the message, too busy working on his Caliburn.

96042887.title: Ace Combat AV edition
summary: Military radio chatter captures a purple-and-black AFV rolling through an active strike zone, taking out an AV with its main gun while performing a mid-air barrel roll. The scouts confirm they are not hallucinating.

96044510.title: Gamer reset Magical girl Go!
summary: A 'Gamer System' notification letter informs Motoko of a server crash requiring her to reset -- this time as a magical girl. The letter apologizes for the inconvenience and assures her the transformation stick is complimentary.

96048203.title: Blackwall Admin
summary: Motoko deep dives toward the Blackwall and watches a rogue AI get shredded by a coordinated daemon swarm controlled by a humanoid figure with yellow segmented eyes. She retreats to Lizzies, tells Yoko she's done with deep diving, and starts planning an EMP mega bomb just in case.

96056438.title: Seattle Citinet
summary: A Seattle CitiNet greentext post from a Cougar Claws ganger recounts a random NC girl walking into their territory, laughing at the gang name, flatlining a Rat gang lord and his guards mid-negotiation, asking for directions to the nearest ramen joint, stealing the dead guy's car, and bouncing.

96056948.title: Motoko tries Nomad Spaghetti
summary: Motoko trades Jun's awful burritos to Panam for a pot of Scorpion's Nomad Special pasta. She's slurping it down with delight until Panam casually mentions the soft, meaty noodles are worms.

96057092.title: Who even uses keychains anymore?
summary: Wakako enjoys a few days of peace while Motoko is out of Night City, only for her agent to flood with frantic messages from Seattle. Audio files capture gangers screaming, singing nursery rhymes while sobbing, and begging Wakako to call off whatever cyberpsycho she sent. Motoko picks up the phone sounding completely casual.

96057104.title: Maximum effort in Seattle
summary: A Deadpool-referencing merc named Wade Wilson stumbles into Seattle and crosses paths with the purple-haired try-hard. Maximum effort ensues.

96059555.title: Arasaka Academy defenses
summary: Arasaka Academy's head of internal security Sun Lee runs a three-day stakeout with multiple tactical teams to catch Motoko on her next break-in. They detect a network intrusion, scramble all teams, breach an office -- only to discover it was an employee watching porn. Intel confirms Motoko left town a week ago. The entire operation was for nothing.

96061328.title: Poor Liam, the strangest thing happened.
summary: An SPD officer finds Liam standing over Franco's corpse. Liam insists a random girl killed Franco, which the officer finds implausible -- until he considers this is Night City.

96063590.title: But you promised!
summary: Motoko returns from a Seattle errand and makes Panam promise not to be mad or ask how. She then reveals a stolen car with a rocket launcher in the trunk. Panam screams. Motoko reminds her she promised.

96069089.title: NC kid in Seattle
summary: Sam moves from Westbrook to Seattle and tries to pay protection money to the local 9 Ballers gang. The moment he mentions he's from Night City, all three kids trip over themselves to waive the fee, welcome him, and sprint away.

96069524.title: Even Stronger than You Could Know
summary: Motoko finds the best ramen shop in Seattle -- a filthy dive called the Slurp'n Hot run by an old man named Montrose who doesn't flinch at the blood on her boots. She geeks out over his ancient jukebox, drops eddies on quarters to play classical music, and he grudgingly decides he likes her.

96069689.title: Seattle: A little off but still familiar
summary: Motoko test-drives a Saeder-Krupp car she stole in Seattle and marvels at how different it feels from her Quadra. The quiet ride through unfamiliar but recognizable city blocks hits different from Night City's chaos.

96088637.title: The H-word.
summary: Motoko modifies Jun's gun to look ridiculous and delivers an unbroken string of hand puns. Jun smacks her over the head and demands she fix it. Motoko keeps going.

96127739.title: Post Chap 126
summary: After the Seattle delivery gig, Motoko cheerfully reveals to Panam that there was a hidden sniper, extra men in cars, and she killed three people and stole a ride. Panam suggests they play the quiet game for the rest of the drive back to NC.

96129332.title: The Bandit
summary: Malcolm begs Motoko for another job to pay for his Caliburn repairs after getting dropped by insurance. She makes him do bizarre vending machine heists as driving practice, and he earns the street name 'The Vending Machine Bandit' after BDs of his Rayfield dragging a Burrito XXL machine go viral.

96130715.title: Captain´s Log
summary: A Star Trek poser gang on patrol in Westbrook encounters Motoko, who recognizes their phaser props and drops knowledge about Captain Picard. The captain nearly loses his mind that this alien creature has seen actual episodes and orders an immediate return to HQ with the intel.

96131576.title: Smokey and the Bandit 1
summary: Malcolm calls Ichi over CB radio using Bandit/Yukidaruma callsigns while a Smokey-and-the-Bandit-style chase unfolds. Ichi runs with a convoy through Texas while Malcolm shakes a Texarkana sheriff off his tail.

96133160.title: Bandit 1
summary: Motoko drives backwards at high speed through NCPD, MaxTac, and Arasaka kill teams in a Bandit Express chase while V screams from the back seat about her terminated Arasaka contract. Smasher ends up tagged with graffiti reading 'Bandit 1, Smokey 0.'

96133493.title: Behind the scenes Chapter 126
summary: Three people sit on car hoods near a Seattle coffee joint sipping synth coffee after a narrowly avoided gunfight. The scene is surprisingly relaxed for a gig that almost went sideways.

96138392.title: Bandit 2
summary: Vik works on Jackie's wounds in a modified Delamaine limo while Hiromi directs them to a clinic off Arasaka's radar. V slams her fist on the seat, desperate to save Jackie.

96138590.title: Smokey and the Bandit 2
summary: Sheriff Buford T. Justice III of Texarkana orders a Diablo Sandwich and NiCola at Old Hickory House before getting word that the Bandit is running through his jurisdiction. The Smokey and the Bandit chase continues across the Republic of Texas.

96144932.title: Smokey and the Bandit 3
summary: Malcolm and Ichi coordinate via CB radio as the Bandit run continues through Sulphur Springs and toward Amarillo, dodging the persistent Texarkana sheriff the whole way.

96148961.title: SPD after action
summary: An SPD detective debriefs his chief on the Rizzo Murders in Seattle, noting military chrome in the recovered vehicle. The political implications run deep, and the detective's flexible wording in the report earns praise from the chief.

96192101.title: Smokey and the Bandit 4
summary: Malcolm blows past Wichita Falls checkpoints and links up with 'Rubber Ducky' on the CB to navigate around Sheppard Air Force Base and rejoin Ichi's convoy. The Bandit run continues through the heartland.

96194336.title: A little bit of...
summary: Motoko writes a jingle listing all her chooms outside Section 9 to the tune of 'Mambo No. 5' -- Sasha, V, GearSlot, Hayato, Judy, Panam, and Yoko. Hiromi is not amused that she wasn't included.

96196007.title: Curious Cat and the Kitten
summary: Maine's crew spots a little girl chatting up Sasha at the Afterlife bar. Sasha brushes it off, but the kid left an impression -- and an encrypted contact shard. Maine files it under 'Sasha's weird new fan.'

96196547.title: Cyber'psychos' and Cheating
summary: V props an unconscious Arasaka employee against a wall to look like she's just chilling, chrome joints locked in place, sunglasses on. Johnny's ghost appreciates the craftsmanship of the deception while V smokes to mask the street stink.

96197570.title: Hiromi meets Sasha
summary: Hiromi catches Motoko consulting with Sasha on a job and flips out -- not about the danger, but about being bypassed as Section 9's manager and money person. Sasha watches the meltdown unfold.

96197882.title: Catgirl Hiromi is on the case!
summary: Hiromi hears Motoko was chatting up a catgirl at Afterlife and spirals into planning how to become a catgirl herself, convinced Motoko has a type. Motoko just wanted Sasha's help with a Biotechnica case.

96237407.title: Hiromi's parents discipline their daughter
summary: Hiromi's parents formally schedule a kitchen table meeting to discuss her portfolio diversification and asset management. Their corpo parenting style treats their daughter's mercenary side-gig finances like a quarterly earnings report.

96249509.title: Simulated Capital Assumed Valuations
summary: An Arasaka Academy class covers 'Simulated Capital Assumed Valuations' -- how to invest in private companies using creative financial instruments. The propaganda-grade educational material is delivered via tinny voiceover and sepia-toned graphics.

96251972.title: Three pears clan meeting
summary: The entire Mitsunashi clan holds their annual reunion in a formal conference room, wearing suits, reviewing the family's yearly earnings and losses. Hiromi's corpo family treats holiday gatherings like shareholder meetings.

96265478.title: You are such a gonk Motoko
summary: Sasha finds notes reading 'Don't be a self-sacrificing gonk!' plastered on her door, car, agent, and fridge for weeks. She traces the stalker to Motoko, who refuses to stop until Sasha promises to value her own life.

96268688.title: We call him the Oni!
summary: Top Gear-style narration introduces Jun 'The Oni' through absurd rumors: he sheds his skin like a snake, his first name is literally 'The,' he's banned from Las Vegas, and brake lights come on in his buttocks when he slows down.

96269255.title: Jun POV
summary: Jun holds his little sister in a rare embrace and reflects on his promise to their mother to look after her. His still-flesh heart squeezes at how vibrant and full of life Motoko is, even as she squirms with embarrassment.

96273464.title: A fountain of wisdom
summary: Motoko stares at pages of garbage code for a system crash quickhack and despairs. Jun, eating a burrito with his eyes closed, accidentally drops zen wisdom about starting at the beginning. Motoko uses the advice without realizing Jun was just savoring his food.

96286088.title: The Oni Myths
summary: Maelstrom gangers kidnap Jun during a midnight burrito run and argue about whether he's really the Oni. They trade increasingly absurd rumors about him -- that he eats chrome, that his eyes can melt steel -- while Jun sits there listening.

96300320.title: Talk-No-Jutsu
summary: Hiromi calls a Section 9 meeting focused on Malcolm, who squirms under the interrogation. The crew sits in their latest base around a steel table, shrouded in dim lighting, as Hiromi grills Malcolm about his spending habits and bargaining skills in full corpo mode.

96307286.title: Metabolism
summary: Motoko hits Body 10 -- the human limit -- and immediately flexes in front of a gym mirror, pushing for the superhuman threshold. She ignores everyone calling her name, lost in the rush of stats going beyond what meat should allow.

96315710.title: Professor Kusanagi, PhD
summary: Motoko spends hours trying to prettify Section 9's server entrance and curses artists who hard-coded measurements in bananas. The perfectionism spiral ends with her flipping a virtual table and considering just writing the whole thing off.

96317858.title: Motoko Intro to Bioretics
summary: Motoko arrives at a hidden lab disguised as a storage shed, guarded by cloaked quad-.50-cal turrets. Inside, she begins her introduction to bioretics -- the fusion of biology and cybernetics that could change everything about how she builds chrome.

96337523.title: Sasha-Sensei
summary: Maine sits in an Afterlife booth for an hour watching Sasha teach a kindergarten-age Motoko netrunning, the kid's conversation peppered with purring, meowing, and 'nya.' He stares at his empty shot glasses and questions his life choices.

96346535.title: Nyan Nyan Nyannnn
summary: Sasha asks Motoko to customize a quickhack with a flying cat display as her signature. Motoko sighs and builds it. The 'rainbow cat of doom' spreads through Night City's netrunning scene, reportedly causing psychosis in some hacked gonks.

96384737.title: Clucken Seattle
summary: Motoko and Panam hit the Night City checkpoint on the way back from Seattle. International arrivals processing means going inside instead of drive-through, and the faded posters extolling the City of Dreams set the tone for coming home.

96399071.title: Mr. Anderson
summary: Motoko cosplays as an Agent from The Matrix, stalking a solo named Thomas through an empty subway station with a smoking Desert Eagle, calling him 'Mr. Anderson.' Thomas is not amused by the kid's movie references.

96404057.title: Manual override
summary: Motoko tells Sasha she'll meet someone named Lucy in the future, then claims she 'hacked time' to explain the precognition. When pressed, she jumps out a window to escape the conversation.

96411995.title: A gift from the Lauging man and the Italian Xmas Donkey
summary: N54 News reports the Laughing Man has struck again -- BioTechnica's entire local board goes into emergency psychiatric hospitalization after their OS, agents, home security, and transportation all get hacked simultaneously. A donkey-themed holiday surprise is somehow involved.

96426953.title: DOOM runs on anything
summary: Motoko creates a quickhack that runs DOOM on security robots, using the classic game as a viral payload. Sasha is both horrified and impressed as enemy bots start playing E1M1 while their systems crash.

96427574.title: Saint Wick
summary: V finds a gold-labeled box in a Dogtown weapons shop that makes Johnny Silverhand go quiet. The inscription references John Wick, and the contents suggest someone built a saint's shrine to the legend of the Baba Yaga in Night City.

96430775.title: Mr Anderson or should I call you Mr Wick? Part 1
summary: Motoko faces down a gunbarrel held by Thomas Anderson -- a solo on a hit job. Hiromi sits on the couch eating snacks while they negotiate. The standoff blurs the lines between The Matrix and John Wick references.

96452525.title: You can never take a reference too far!
summary: The Matrix and John Wick crossover references spiral out of control as Motoko, Thomas, and V trade quips that layer movie callbacks on top of each other until nobody can tell which franchise they're living in.

96453950.title: Arachnophobia
summary: Motoko discovers she has arachnophobia when a spider drone malfunctions and chases her through the workshop. The girl who faces down borgs and cyberpsychos without flinching screams at eight legs.

96464546.title: She Walks
summary: Motoko walks through Night City streets and the narration captures how her presence registers differently to everyone she passes -- threat, curiosity, irrelevance -- depending on what chrome they're packing and what neighborhood they're in.

96469442.title: Fake-out!
summary: Motoko sets up an elaborate fake-out to trick an enemy into thinking she's coming in the front door while she's already inside. The misdirection works flawlessly until she trips over a mop bucket in the dark.

96469550.title: New Game Plus
summary: Motoko's Gamer system resets with a 'New Game Plus' notification. She wakes up back at the beginning of her Night City life with all her stats and perks intact, ready to speedrun everything she did the first time.

96469973.title: Scavs a the untold story
summary: A scav tells his side of the story -- they were just doing their grisly job when some purple-haired demon came through the wall. The untold perspective of the nameless cannon fodder Motoko chews through.

96482414.title: On A Razr's Edge
summary: Motoko balances on a razor's edge between cold professionalism and the thrill of violence. The line between merc work and cyberpsychosis blurs as she pushes deeper into the combat zone.

96492257.title: style
summary: Motoko's style and fashion sense get examined -- how she mixes reinforced combat gear with casual streetwear, creating an aesthetic that's part merc, part teenager, and entirely unsettling to anyone who knows what the clothing actually does.

96499988.title: Hiromi's Check In
summary: Hiromi checks in on Motoko after a rough gig. The conversation is casual on the surface but loaded with concern underneath as Hiromi reads between the lines of Motoko's too-cheerful deflections.

96544913.title: Motoko Slim
summary: An alternate take where Motoko's body type is slim and small, emphasizing the disconnect between her tiny frame and the terrifying amount of damage she can deal. Enemies keep underestimating the kid.

96547157.title: Motoko's family tree
summary: Someone maps out Motoko's family tree and connections -- Jun, the Tyger Claws through Fujimura, Hayato, Hiromi's corpo family, Panam's Aldecaldos -- and realizes the web of alliances she's accidentally built is staggering.

96563843.title: The Matrix
summary: Motoko references The Matrix one too many times and finds herself actually jacked into a construct that looks suspiciously like the white void. She's not sure if it's a netrunning glitch or her Gamer system having a laugh.

96568598.title: The Motoshow Episode 69 (Nice)
summary: Episode 69 of the Motoshow broadcasts, covering Motoko's latest exploits with the usual mix of breathless commentary, viewer call-ins, and highlight reels of things that should not be physically possible.

96577238.title: Can't catch a break!
summary: Motoko can't catch a break -- every time she tries to relax, another crisis, gig, or emergency drags her back into action. The universe refuses to let her eat her noodles in peace.

96590669.title: Not a good sign
summary: Something is off and Motoko can feel it. Small signs stack up -- a camera that shouldn't be there, a face she's seen twice, a delivery van parked too long. The paranoia that keeps her alive kicks into gear.

96596579.title: Monkey around
summary: Motoko's monkeylike agility gets showcased as she bounces between buildings, pipes, and fire escapes in a vertical chase through Night City's architecture. Anyone watching from the street would think she was a cyberpsycho.

96620657.title: Time to chrome the fuck up (C)
summary: The crew decides it's time to chrome up seriously. Motoko helps Section 9 pick out upgrades, matching each member's fighting style to the right cyberware while Hiromi runs the numbers on cost.

96625469.title: YOU DID THIS
summary: Motoko's actions have consequences that cascade outward. Someone she wronged, saved, or ignored comes back with a grudge, a debt, or information that changes everything. The 'YOU DID THIS' hits different when she can't deny it.

96626294.title: Section 9 commercial
summary: Motoko sits on the apartment couch flipping channels with her optics, bored out of her skull. Nothing but ads on every station. She debates whether any entertainment in 2077 is worth watching or if she should just go find some scavs to flatten.

96633092.title: Ship of Theseus
summary: V wakes up on Vik's chair after extensive surgery, half-paralyzed and on the good drugs. Vik tells her to use her healing ability while he cleans organic chunks off his ripper hand. The Ship of Theseus question hangs in the air -- how much of V is still V.

96694880.title: Choose your own adventure
summary: Sasha field-tests Motoko's new 'Taser Quickhack' and word spreads through the gangs about a Short Circuit hack that leaves whole crews spasming on the ground. What starts as a rumor becomes a Choose Your Own Adventure of escalating chaos.

96703394.title: Uchiha Motoko
summary: Hiromi returns to find Motoko has gone full Uchiha -- quoting Sasuke lines, wearing a dark outfit, and brooding. Jun confirms it's dumb even by Motoko standards. The Naruto references will not stop.

96768941.title: B-sides
summary: Rebecca power-walks up to Motoko at a gig, demanding to know why she wasn't told about the side mission. The B-sides chapter fills in scenes from between main story beats, with Rebecca's POV driving the action.

96846620.title: Tachi-Gear, the leading in Solo Survival!
summary: Jun and Hiromi walk into the Kusanagi apartment to find it packed floor-to-ceiling with gadgets. Motoko barely notices them as she welds circuitry into spy-film devices -- Section 9's own Q-branch is born, branded Tachi-Gear.

96853631.title: Big Iron Theme
summary: A parody of 'Big Iron' by Marty Robbins retells Motoko's encounter with Texas Red. The NC Purple Ranger rides into town, and the outlaw learns too late that two dozen dead men weren't enough warning.

96854858.title: Back to the future!
summary: Motoko sprints past Jun dragging a pale Hiromi by the hand, a broken katana blade lodged in Hiromi's shoulder, screaming about there being no time machine and seeing nothing. Jun stares at the burning wreck she left behind.

96901109.title: Cursed with knowledge
summary: Years in the future, a merc crew hits a fancy corpo-district club and stumbles into a performance by an older Motoko. The narrator realizes too late that the casual singer on stage is the same ghost who haunts Night City's underworld.

96929039.title: Social Link Go!
summary: Motoko bonds with Malcolm and a Persona-style Social Link notification fires -- 'Thou art I, and I am Thou.' She panics as her Gamer system starts assigning Arcana cards to her friendships.

96933518.title: Jun's gift
summary: Motoko gifts Jun a custom kanabo for his birthday -- a baseball bat modded with thermal katana heating and an electrical discharge mode that can stun borgs. Jun lights it up and it erupts into a flaming war club.

96942086.title: S-social Link Go?
summary: Motoko's Social Link system expands as she levels bonds with increasingly dangerous people, including Dexter DeShawn who exploits every free meal. Each rank reveals uncomfortable truths about her connections.

96948470.title: Wild Tachikoma
summary: Tachikoma Rod screeches and zig-zags to avoid being put in the Garage of Useless Transport while Malcolm chases her. Noir the black-and-white Tachikoma mutters death threats at Malcolm. The spider-bots have personalities and opinions now.

96951806.title: I challenge you to a childrens card game!
summary: Motoko discovers the near-total absence of video games in 2077 and decides to fix it. She researches gaming history, finding that the industry collapsed and never recovered, then starts planning to bring back card games and consoles.

96953729.title: Cardboard boxes
summary: Arasaka Academy stockroom clerk Ishigawa grumbles about his demotion while searching for gold-embossed pens. He stumbles on something he shouldn't have -- a cardboard box that definitely wasn't there before. Metal Gear Solid energy radiates from the scene.

96996653.title: Motoko the Loot Goblin
summary: Motoko sulks about a gig that went sideways. Hiromi assumes she's upset about the fixer who screwed them -- but Motoko's actually mad they found a stash of loot they couldn't carry. The loot goblin priorities are unshakable.

96998189.title: What if! Motoko took after Snake instead
summary: Motoko channels Snake from Metal Gear, activating TRUE STEALTH mode via a big red button on her watch. She crawls through a Wraith compound in a cardboard box while their leader explains his master plan to oblivious underlings.

96998657.title: Grofit
summary: Hiromi tallies the spoils from a Wraith camp -- free guns, ammo, maxdocs, and vehicle salvage. Section 9's next couple of gigs are pre-funded. Hiromi's expense reports have never been easier.

97070540.title: Post infiltration chat log
summary: Motoko drops good-news-bad-news in the Section 9 chat: bad news is her infiltration discovered info that could trigger a fifth corporate war. Good news is her stealth run was so clean an Arasaka cyber ninja would applaud.

97072820.title: Motoko remembered. Always be Prepared.
summary: Motoko earns the scariest rep in the Badlands after a group of teenagers demolished a Wraith camp that even Dakota wouldn't touch. The nomad fixer files Motoko under 'nuclear option' and adjusts her threat assessment accordingly.

97082504.title: Tripping over trouble
summary: Motoko and the crew stumble into a neglected corridor while arguing about an XBD that mocked Section 9. What they find in the dark changes the stakes of their next mission.

97090259.title: Mimi
summary: Hiromi worries after Motoko goes no-contact for days, locked in her room working on a breakthrough. Jun confirms she's alive but manic. When Motoko finally emerges, she's built something that makes Hiromi's jaw drop.

97149715.title: Bloody Aftermath (Dakota's POV)
summary: Dakota's crew arrives at the aftermath of Motoko's Wraith raid. The fixer, old enough to remember The Red's mass graves, stands in ash and blood while her younger men grip their weapons with white knuckles. The scale of carnage is something they weren't prepared for.

97151623.title: Fixer chat
summary: A secure fixer chat between Dakota, Wakako, and others debates whether the Motoko Kusanagi Solo Profile is accurate. Each fixer verifies the jobs they've seen firsthand. The consensus: the file isn't overblown, she really is that dangerous.

97152733.title: Smasher realizes the 'truth'
summary: Adam Smasher crashes through a wall, grabs Motoko by the throat, and accuses her of being Morgan Blackhand in disguise. The sneaky mayhem, the murder, the music, the anime character name -- it all adds up to Smasher. Motoko can barely breathe, let alone correct him.

97155058.title: That's two things
summary: Hiromi asks what Motoko did to the Minotaur. She added hands. Many hands. 'When your robot has a craving for hands' is not an explanation Hiromi accepts. Motoko admits she stabs gonks and builds handy-centipedes, 'so that's two things.'

97162081.title: Minotaur 1S custom
summary: Motoko mods the looted Minotaur mech into something bigger and meaner while Section 9 watches from the elevator. Sparks fly as she darts around the frame, fitting custom components into a war machine that shouldn't exist in a teenage girl's garage.

97163533.title: After Action Report
summary: Thomas Anderson patches himself up in a safehouse after losing a fight against Motoko at his target's apartment. The war vet turned solo reviews what went wrong and realizes he's now on Arasaka's radar. The kid was faster than he expected.

97164481.title: My Mom is a Walking Death Machine
summary: Gloria Martinez, mortally wounded and rebuilt into a Minotaur combat frame, stomps up to Arasaka Academy to pick up David. Instead of a lockdown, the students just giggle. David's mom is now a walking death machine and he could not be more embarrassed.

97207528.title: "No, I can't possibly be an Idol, It's just a gig!"
summary: An older Motoko tours as a pop star without realizing she's a pop star. Hiromi manages the chaos while Motoko crushes hearts at every venue, oblivious to her own idol status because 'it's just a gig.'

97208272.title: Crazy City Folk
summary: Dakota works on a recovered Wraith vehicle and reflects on how she got pulled into this mess. The city kids Section 9 brought more firepower and chaos to the Badlands than she expected, and she's getting too soft to refuse Saul's requests.

97208710.title: Kotomo dealing with a sniper.
summary: Motoko stands on a bus singing her heart out at a Kotomo event while tracking a sniper across the street through her optics. She times her dodge to the music, never breaking character, and her Tachikoma buddies close in on the shooter's position.

97215934.title: You Cracks
summary: Motoko refuses to cover Pon Pon Shit despite a decade of the song's inexplicable popularity. No amount of fancy hotel suites will make her play it. She gets along with the actual Us Cracks members but draws the line at their biggest hit.

97222915.title: The Most Interesting Choom in Night City
summary: A Dos Equis-style 'Most Interesting Choom in Night City' narration plays over scenes of Motoko befriending an Arasaka CI agent, scouting in the Badlands, and casually accomplishing impossible feats. The narrator treats everything as totally normal.

97225045.title: Negaverse Quest: Valerie in the City
summary: An alternate universe where V arrives in Night City and runs into Motoko's section of the story from a Negaverse angle, experiencing the same events from a radically different perspective.

97252888.title: Business Practices
summary: Motoko's business practices come under scrutiny as Hiromi tries to professionalize Section 9's operations. The gap between 'we kill scavs for free' and 'we need to invoice properly' creates friction.

97284139.title: Too many Ghosts
summary: Too many ghosts haunt Night City's net -- and Motoko might be responsible for several of them. Her quickhacks leave traces that netrunners keep stumbling over, creating an urban legend about the girl who lives in the wires.

97286137.title: Neko-Chan upgrade
summary: Neko-Chan gets an upgrade. Motoko tinkers with her cat-themed branding, adding new features to the persona she's built around Sasha's influence. The line between joke and identity keeps blurring.

97300666.title: Inhuman thoughts
summary: Motoko catches herself thinking in ways that aren't quite human anymore -- processing combat data too fast, categorizing people by threat level before saying hello. The inhuman thoughts creep in between heartbeats.

97308820.title: Memetic hazards
summary: Motoko's songs, hacks, and reputation are spreading like memetic hazards. People who encounter her work can't stop thinking about it, sharing it, or being changed by it. Night City has a new virus and it plays guitar.

97310611.title: What is an Annihilation Build. V POV.
summary: V's POV on what an Annihilation build looks like in practice -- all shotgun, all carnage, no subtlety. The contrast between V's brute-force approach and Motoko's surgical precision highlights different schools of Night City survival.

97313944.title: Motoko 'fights' smasher
summary: Motoko faces Adam Smasher in combat. The fight pushes both combatants past their limits as chrome meets chrome in a clash that shakes the building. Every trick in Motoko's arsenal gets tested against Arasaka's ultimate weapon.

97317364.title: Motoko's supervised Gemini install
summary: Motoko undergoes a supervised Gemini full-body conversion with her friends watching the process. The surgery is meticulous, the anxiety is real, and the first moments in a new body redefine what she is.

97367227.title: ACME brand Fully Usable Bodies And Remodeling™ machine
summary: An ACME-branded 'Fully Usable Bodies And Remodeling' machine promises full-body cyborg conversion with cartoon logic. The Looney Tunes energy clashes with Night City's grim reality in exactly the way Motoko would enjoy.

97371463.title: SUB-WAY 2077
summary: A Subway sandwich shop in 2077 Night City somehow still exists, serving the same questionable ingredients with the same broken soda fountain. Motoko orders a footlong and contemplates how some things survive even the apocalypse.

97432711.title: Post Afterlife Chat Log
summary: Post-Afterlife chat log between Section 9 members dissects what just happened at the legendary merc bar. The casual text format captures the crew's dynamic -- Hiromi managing, Ichi deadpanning, Malcolm panicking, and Motoko deflecting.

97432876.title: Vik Files
summary: Vik's patient files on Motoko read like a horror novel from a medical perspective. The ripperdoc catalogs her injuries, recoveries, and the increasingly impossible things her body does between visits.

97502353.title: Constant Vigilance!
summary: Constant vigilance defines Motoko's daily routine -- scanning rooftops, checking sight lines, monitoring frequencies. The paranoia isn't paranoia when people actually are trying to kill you.

97502821.title: The "Top" Dog
summary: Motoko establishes herself as Night City's top dog through sheer accumulated reputation. The quiet moment where every fixer, gang leader, and corpo acknowledges her threat level marks a turning point.

97503277.title: Motoko "Super" Spy
summary: Motoko goes full super spy, infiltrating a target with gadgets, disguises, and tradecraft that would make James Bond jealous. The mission requires finesse instead of firepower for once.

97604977.title: Section 9 Training Session #1
summary: Section 9 runs their first proper training session. Motoko puts the crew through drills that push them beyond what street mercs normally handle, exposing gaps in their coordination.

97824955.title: The New Intern
summary: A new intern arrives at Section 9's operations and discovers that 'entry-level merc work' involves more casual violence and burrito logistics than any corpo job posting prepared them for.

97825777.title: Tachi-what-ma?
summary: Someone tries to explain what a Tachikoma is and fails spectacularly. The spider-bot concept doesn't translate well to people who haven't seen one crawl across a ceiling while humming show tunes.

97830160.title: Mama bear Gloria
summary: Gloria Martinez goes full mama bear when she learns what her son David has been doing with Section 9. The protective instinct of a Night City mother meets the reality that her kid chose this life.

97835218.title: I'm okay!
summary: Motoko insists she's okay after a rough job. Nobody believes her. The gap between what she says and what her friends can see drives the quiet tension of a crew that knows its leader too well.

97842271.title: After David and Hiromi Talk
summary: After David and Hiromi talk, the implications of their conversation ripple through Section 9. David's perspective on the crew shifts as Hiromi's corpo pragmatism meets his street-kid idealism.

97848676.title: Cybermommyborg
summary: Motoko's full-borg mothering instincts manifest as she fusses over her crew's chrome, health, and eating habits with the intensity of a cyborg helicopter parent. The term 'cybermommyborg' gets coined.

97893703.title: Kusanagi Swagger
summary: Motoko walks into a room and the Kusanagi swagger is palpable. The confidence of a girl who knows she can kill everyone present but chooses to smile instead puts an entire building on edge.

97896604.title: Memetic hairzard
summary: Motoko's hairstyle becomes a memetic hazard -- people who see it can't stop thinking about it. Purple hair with that specific cut starts appearing on gonks across Night City trying to copy the look.

97909690.title: The Weird One
summary: Everyone agrees Motoko is the weird one. Not dangerous-weird, not cyberpsycho-weird, just fundamentally off in a way that makes even hardened mercs do a double-take. She takes it as a compliment.

97926580.title: Martinez Family Cooperation
summary: The Martinez family cooperates with Section 9 as Gloria, David, and the crew find common ground. The working relationship between a protective mother and teenage mercenaries is exactly as awkward as it sounds.

97963108.title: 20 Years later.
summary: Twenty years later, Motoko looks back on everything that happened. Night City has changed and so has she, but the ghost in the machine is still running, still fighting, still playing guitar on rooftops.

97964263.title: Resin must flow, Resin for the Resin gods!
summary: Motoko needs resin for her 3D printing projects and goes on a supply run that escalates into a full procurement operation. 'Resin must flow' becomes the battle cry as she strip-mines every available source.

97966795.title: Four Words.
summary: Four words change everything. A short, punchy piece where a single sentence recontextualizes a relationship, a mission, or a threat level. Brevity hits harder than a full mag.

98030755.title: Kindred Spirits
summary: Motoko and a kindred spirit bond over shared weirdness. Two people who don't quite fit Night City's mold find common ground in being the odd ones out in a world of chrome and violence.

98238295.title: Seras is a gonk, and forgot about agents.
summary: Seras forgot about agents -- the personal communication devices everyone carries in 2077. A plot hole gets lampshaded as someone points out that Motoko could have just called instead of doing whatever convoluted thing she did.

98250031.title: Your Business is My Business by Josh Kavorkin - Biotechnica Scandal
summary: Josh Kavorkin's business exposé covers a Biotechnica scandal from the lens of Night City's media landscape. The article reads like investigative journalism filtered through corpo-controlled press.

98299963.title: Cyber Ninjas Make Great Friends
summary: Cyber ninjas make great friends, as it turns out. Motoko's infiltration skills attract like-minded operatives who appreciate the craft. Professional respect between sneaky people transcends faction lines.

98319184.title: Kusanagi Instincts
summary: Motoko's Kusanagi instincts fire before her conscious mind catches up -- dodging a bullet she didn't see, drawing a weapon she didn't decide to draw. The reflexes are faster than thought now.

98458069.title: Bulletproof Becca
summary: Rebecca discovers she's bulletproof -- or at least, the chrome Motoko helped her install is. The moment of realization when a round pings off her chest instead of punching through it changes Becca's entire approach to combat.

98470462.title: There is no escape from fighting.
summary: Motoko can't escape fighting. Every quiet moment, every rest stop, every noodle shop visit ends with someone starting trouble. She's beginning to suspect Night City's violence is gravitationally attracted to her.

98481175.title: Odd Investigations
summary: An insurance investigator and adjustor review security footage of a truck that didn't clip the gate like the usual SCSM driver. The odd investigation uncovers that someone far more competent than expected was behind the wheel.

98492794.title: Section 9, Money management.
summary: Motoko zones out during a Section 9 meeting while Hiromi handles money negotiations. The crew's financial management reveals the gap between Motoko's combat skills and her total disinterest in eddies.

98496706.title: 「Symbols Between Seconds」
summary: Time becomes something tangible and strange for Motoko as her perception accelerates beyond normal human experience. 'Symbols Between Seconds' captures the moments that exist in the gaps between heartbeats and trigger pulls.

98531515.title: Thirty thou.
summary: Rebecca lies on her stripped bed running the numbers -- thirty thousand eddies from the last gig, shared rent with Pilar, the cost of chrome and fashionware. The economics of being a merc in Night City laid bare from Becca's POV.

98532121.title: Maximum Mike voice of the people
summary: Maximum Mike broadcasts a burning question on Morro Rock Radio: a NUSA military officer with experimental Militech chrome flatlined dozens in Corpo Plaza before MaxTac took him down. The chrome vanished. Mike wants answers.

98534368.title: Damn Motoko-brain
summary: Motoko's 'Motoko-brain' -- the part of her that makes terrible impulsive decisions -- strikes again. No amount of chrome or intelligence stats can fix the fundamental problem of being Motoko.

98597299.title: PHO Blacksmithery
summary: A PHO (Parahumans Online style) forum thread on the Net City Runners boards discusses blacksmithing and custom weapon crafting. The online community dissects Motoko's gear with the enthusiasm of dedicated hobbyists.

98629681.title: Followers of the Driving BD
summary: Synth and his crew sit in Kaneda's Quadra ready to roll on a job, but nobody moves. They're all watching a BD of Motoko driving, unable to look away from the sheer skill and insanity of her behind the wheel.

98679016.title: How did you know?
summary: Sasha asks Motoko how she knew to give her that baby short circuit program that saved her life. Motoko deflects with 'it just made sense,' unable to explain her precognitive meta-knowledge without sounding insane.

98685742.title: Rebecca and Maine talk.
summary: Rebecca confronts Maine about the crew's direction after Sasha drags Motoko away. The conversation between Rebecca and the big man touches on loyalty, crew dynamics, and what happens when a kid starts outperforming the adults.

98710729.title: 2 Scavs
summary: Two scavs chat while harvesting chrome from bodies. One finds neat Kiroshi optics; the other wonders if they should worry about that purple-haired girl the forums keep mentioning. They decide she's probably just an urban legend.

98722885.title: S-9
summary: Section 9 celebrates their newest member at an entertainment center festival. Rebecca brings surprisingly great beer. Ichi feels great about the crew's unity, even as Motoko and Becca argue about who's the better drinker.

98745655.title: For My Honour, Maine-Taichō!
summary: Rebecca formally challenges Maine for the right to join Section 9, citing better benefits and dental. What starts as a joke escalates into a comedic honor duel with anime-style narration and Maine looking confused.

98757877.title: Burnout
summary: Someone rants at Maine about how bones actually break and why his reckless chrome usage is physically destroying him. The medical reality of cyberpsychosis gets laid out in terms even a meathead can understand.

98766313.title: Maine ponders
summary: An essay-style analysis of Maine, Rebecca, and Lucy's character arcs examines why Seras writes them the way she does. Maine's internal struggle, Becca's hidden depth, and Lucy's guarded nature all get unpacked.

98770099.title: Cliffs of Dover
summary: Alice finishes a recording session and gets the studio for an hour to mess around. When Jun checks in 27 minutes later, she's playing Cliffs of Dover with enough skill to make the producers stop and listen.

98865505.title: Thinking of Hiromi
summary: Motoko tells Hiromi she thought of her during a mission. Hiromi's heart skips until Motoko clarifies that Mental Image Hiromi scolded her about money. On a related note, Hiromi should expect a call from some music people.

99034913.title: My Sister
summary: A doctor tries to convince Jun that Motoko likely won't wake up with memories after her injuries. Jun sits vigil by his sister's limp form, remembering their mother's words: 'Look after her, Jun.'

99069212.title: Ichi's no good morning followup
summary: Motoko texts Hiromi that boys are dumb, then reveals Ichi has been keeping the Minotaur in his backyard garage secured with a padlock. Hiromi's rage at the security breach echoes through the text chain.

99080384.title: Happy Profitable Accidents or (I just wanted to show them more music, not this?!)
summary: Motoko tries to show people old music and accidentally creates a profitable enterprise. The 'Happy Profitable Accidents' pile up as Hiromi pivots every cultural blunder into a business opportunity.

99086315.title: Ready Runner Racer.
summary: Motoko and Sasha jack into a virtual street race, Sasha driving a neon pink electric catgirl avatar while Motoko rides shotgun. The netspace race is all style and speed, with actual combat mixed into the laps.

99127283.title: Jun/Motoko bad end
summary: A bad-end alternate timeline where Jun didn't rescue Motoko from Maelstrom in time. Months later, Regina Jones sends V to handle a cyberpsycho sighting -- and the psycho is someone V recognizes.

99264500.title: Spare
summary: Vik removes a cyberdeck that's partially welded to its ports using a rotary cutter. The ripperdoc mutters about how 'I think it doesn't work' was an understatement while salvaging what he can from the mess.

99267638.title: Passenger
summary: Motoko has survived death, dismemberment, and Jun's insect-based food. But today is officially the worst day of her life: something so terrible Ichi can't even console her. The drama is pure Motoko.

99285284.title: Slow Climb
summary: Jackie Welles takes on small gigs around Heywood, slowly climbing the merc ladder. The hustle is real -- no shortcuts, no fancy chrome, just a big guy with bigger ambitions grinding his way up.

99332462.title: Hiromi best selling author
summary: Hiromi becomes a bestselling author with 'Loverunners,' a romance novel that Motoko hasn't read yet. The corpo heiress turned merc manager turned published writer adds another line to her resume.

99408284.title: The greatest loss of Sasha.
summary: Sasha meets with Yoko to clear the air. As the meeting ends, Yoko drops a bomb: Motoko was her apprentice first. Sasha's cat-ear avatar wilts as she realizes she's been out-netrunner'd in the mentorship game.

99432398.title: This is what Rock and Roll looks like
summary: Alice's Violent Hemorrhage band jams with Jun while Motoko watches and gets dragged into performing. The rock and roll session showcases what raw, unpolished music sounds like in Night City's underground scene.

99573488.title: Drycleaning
summary: Motoko's 'time sensitive' gig from Wakako turns out to be picking up the fixer's dry cleaning before the shop closes. She stands in a shop reeking of cleaning supplies, tapping her foot, wondering how she got here.

99589910.title: Full Murder Hobo
summary: An alternate timeline where Motoko never stopped her near-cyberpsycho XP grind against scavs. Full Murder Hobo Motoko is a walking apocalypse that even Night City's worst fear.

99594188.title: Hammer Birthday
summary: Jackie dared Motoko to take down ten scav dens with only a hammer while dressed as a clown. The resurgence of the Bozos makes the evening news. Hiromi and Misty give Jackie the coldest stare in Night City history.

99623408.title: Wrong Gig?
summary: Wakako finishes a dead drop and heads inside while Motoko, four blocks away, realizes something is off about the gig. The wrong job at the wrong time cascades into complications.

99699380.title: The man with the deep voice
summary: Years in the future, a kid tells an interviewer about watching Jun smash a gonk's head with a giant hammer. The man with the deep voice and the Oni reputation has become Night City folklore.

99740618.title: Johnson Leaddong.
summary: V and Motoko trade spy-movie code names that keep missing. 'Not Q.' 'V.' 'Not for vendetta?' 'Just V.' 'Agent M?' 'Never heard of her.' V gets dubbed Johnson Leaddong and is not happy about it.

99760385.title: Gamer V becomes canon
summary: V wakes up to a floating Gamer menu after Vik's surgery. Stats, Health, Rockerboy class -- V has the system now. Vik blames it on Arasaka chrome withdrawal. The chrome came from Section 9.

99763190.title: Gamerization
summary: Motoko reaches a high enough programming skill to interface with her hidden nanites and make the Gamer system visible. Then she figures out replication. Vik, Misty, and everyone else starts leveling up. Misty cheers at level two.

99796580.title: I roll to intimidate.
summary: Jun goes shirtless on a scav raid and never gets shot. His chrome body and sheer Oni presence make every ganger freeze instead of fight. Motoko is furious that intimidation works better than her stealth.

99804431.title: Ah yes, "Non lethal XP"
summary: Motoko punches scavs unconscious and Yoko asks what quickhack she used. 'I punched them?' Yoko refuses to believe that Motoko -- a netrunner prodigy -- handles combat with her fists. Non-lethal XP is non-lethal.

99807032.title: After chapter 170 gig
summary: The Valentinos send Wakako a message thanking her for sending a 'specialist team' to handle a family matter. Wakako has no idea what they're talking about and quietly adds another entry to the 'Motoko, what did you DO?' file.

99944095.title: A Day In The Life of a Food Vendor
summary: A day in the life of a Night City food vendor whose routine gets interrupted by the city's violence. The contrast between mundane commerce and casual gunfire captures daily survival for non-mercs.

99960571.title: UPDATE COMPLETE! MAGIC UNLOCKED!
summary: Motoko's Gamer system pushes an update: Magic Unlocked. She was about to deep dive the Combat Zone when the notification drops. The system keeps adding features she didn't ask for.

100054846.title: Tachi Online for the 'first' time.
summary: Motoko brings her Tachikoma online using a personality matrix based on herself. The cheerful mini-Motoko voice that emerges immediately gives Hiromi a bad feeling about what they've just unleashed.

100055629.title: Brain Bleach
summary: Motoko makes the mistake of asking Wakako a personal question. The fixer reveals she's had five husbands, all without chrome, and the brain bleach Motoko needs doesn't exist.

100056508.title: Black Betty
summary: Judy demands to know why Motoko sent her a BD of doing donuts around Raffen while Black Betty plays. Motoko explains they spoiled her food. The music was just a mood.

100057939.title: Tachikoma development think
summary: Tachikoma's development log shows the little bot's learning process: obstacle detection at 62.8% accuracy, 1,646 wall impacts and counting. Every collision triggers a cheerful 'Uh-Oh' printout.

100060024.title: Juns wardrobe
summary: Jun tries on outfits while Motoko judges each one. Long leather coat: 'Too 1999.' Tank top and jeans: 'Too simple.' Green raincoat: 'Screams serial killer more than everyday killer.'

100065727.title: Uncle Jun
summary: Motoko tells Jun she made a baby. Jun panics until Tachikoma rolls up chirping 'Hi hi, uncle Jun!' Motoko proudly introduces her adorable AI in a spider-bot body. Tachikoma claims she drives better than mommy. Motoko disagrees.

100092880.title: Comforting strength of steel
summary: Outside observers try to parse Motoko's behavior -- the mix of mercy, violence, and absolute chill that makes her incomprehensible to anyone who doesn't know about the Gamer system and Cold Blood perk.

100106818.title: emotional compensation
summary: Jun drops his show and stands up when Motoko walks in looking off. She lost something or someone, and his protective instinct kicks in before she can even explain. The Oni's little sister comes first.

100107556.title: Campaign Smasher
summary: Adam Smasher hacks into an N54 News broadcast to announce his mayoral campaign endorsement of Motoko, calling her the only rocker in Night City worth anything. The news anchor trails off as the borg walks into her shot.

100115914.title: N54 news on Motoko's Mayoral bid
summary: N54 reports on Motoko's mayoral campaign being hit by allegations that her scav-den purges were rigging the daily Body Lotto. Investigative analysts put together statistics that make her kill count look like election fraud.

100142089.title: Contract BD
summary: Motoko gives Judy a new BD to edit -- not combat footage, but herself sitting in a chair while Hiromi negotiates a music deal. Judy can't believe the lack of explosions for once.

100146565.title: Threat Assessment
summary: A bodyguard corp reviews footage of Motoko and runs threat assessments for new hires. The senior bodyguard points to each member of the meeting and asks trainees to evaluate the danger. The kid in the corner scores highest.

100220233.title: Just like old times.
summary: Yuto buzzes with a high he's never felt from Tyger Claw drugs before. The new stuff is lightweight and pleasant -- nothing like the old product. He stumbles to the bathroom, appreciating the effects.

100306378.title: Comm's check
summary: Ichi plans and executes a scav hideout assault with military precision, using the Minotaur and his truck in coordinated positions. The blue-green tactical overlay guides every step. Sometimes even the driver gets to play general.

100392922.title: Runner Rumble Reviews
summary: Runner Rumble Reviews covers Ghost vs Hamm3r. The fight is awful as spectacle -- Hamm3r can't use the minimap and Ghost ends it too quickly. The bookies odds of -300 for Ghost were generous.

100411663.title: Throwing down the Gauntlet
summary: Motoko gets challenged in the netrunner arena and decides to demonstrate what she's actually been practicing. The admin threatening to ban her pauses when she reveals the technique she's developing.

100435561.title: Ghost Over the Horizon
summary: A hypothetical future Motoko with 20s across the board and a custom Gemini frame operates so far beyond normal that she's essentially a force of nature. Ghost Over the Horizon explores what peak Motoko looks like.

100469758.title: Weird that it happened twice
summary: Motoko shoots down a second Kang Tao AV and notes the coincidence. 'If you gave me a penny for every Kang Tao AV I shot down, you'd have two pennies. Which isn't a lot, but weird that it happened twice.'

100472017.title: Falco's drive
summary: Falco's driving gets examined through the lens of the TTRPG rules and his Edgerunners background. The nomad driver's skills translate into a specific kind of expertise that makes him invaluable behind the wheel.

100491829.title: "Shadows of the City" campaign
summary: A tabletop RPG group plays 'Shadows of the City' and the players complain about the overpowered DMNPC -- a teenage girl who solos half the encounters. The GM insists she's balanced.

100558525.title: Netrunner arena update 9.17.2075
summary: The Netrunner Combat Arena posts a new match card with custom BBCode formatting. Admin grudgingly presents Ghost's next opponent with the showmanship of a wrestling promoter.

100641556.title: Density level: Neutron Star
summary: Six months of concerts in and Motoko realizes that performing gains XP too -- more people enjoying the music means more experience. Hiromi drags her to another show. Motoko's numbers go brrr, but not the way she wanted.

100657486.title: All the songs.
summary: Hiromi asks what song Motoko's working on today. Motoko rattles off a dozen options, each one inspiring three pages of reader suggestions. The meta-joke captures how every music chapter generates an avalanche of song recommendations.

100673449.title: Hiromipunk
summary: Hiromi finds a white case on her nightstand labeled 'Gamer.' Inside is a system that starts assigning her stats, skills, and XP. The corpo heiress approaches her new Gamer power with ruthless economic optimization.

100711954.title: Hiromipunk Pt.2 - Points are Money
summary: Hiromi's Gamer system feeds her XP dings in tiny parcels while she works, creating a Skinner box she intellectually recognizes and emotionally cannot resist. She tracks diminishing returns on a spreadsheet.

100713973.title: Stock Market Skill
summary: Hiromi's stock market sixth sense goes haywire at 3 AM. She calls Motoko for intel, convinced something massive is about to shift in the financial sector.

100728256.title: 10 hours after the Arroyo Riots.
summary: Ten hours after the Arroyo Riots, the aftermath settles into the grim routine of Night City cleaning up after itself. Body counts, property damage, and political fallout stack up.

100794184.title: Blackmailing Motoko
summary: Someone tries to blackmail Motoko. The attempt goes about as well as expected -- which is to say, it doesn't go well for the blackmailer at all.

100795636.title: Good riddance
summary: Good riddance to bad chrome. Someone or something gets disposed of, and nobody in Section 9 sheds a tear over it.

100803904.title: Fire Water Burn
summary: Fire, water, and burning -- the elements of a gig that goes hot in every sense. Motoko's approach to collateral damage remains 'technically the building was already on fire when I got here.'

100829083.title: Slave to the Black Gold.
summary: Night City's dependence on synthetic fuel and black gold drives the economy of violence. Motoko gets dragged into a resource war where the commodity is worth more than the lives spent fighting over it.

100834552.title: Over Cool
summary: Motoko's Cool stat hits a threshold where her calm under pressure becomes unsettling to allies and enemies alike. Over Cool means never flinching, never panicking, and never showing the human underneath.

100835143.title: Teenage Mutant Turtles
summary: Motoko encounters something that reminds her of Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles and her brain breaks trying to reconcile 2077 reality with 1980s nostalgia.

100877830.title: Oopsie Daisy
summary: An 'oopsie daisy' from Motoko means someone's day just got significantly worse. The casual way she reports catastrophic collateral damage to Hiromi has become Section 9's most reliable running gag.

100893427.title: Hiromipunk - Contracts are Death
summary: Hiromi's Gamer system reaches the point where contracts are literally death -- each one a calculated risk with XP rewards that her optimization-obsessed brain cannot ignore.

100902553.title: Hiromi's parents are insufferable
summary: Hiromi's parents are insufferable in the way only corpo parents can be -- scheduling family time like board meetings and evaluating their daughter's social connections like portfolio assets.

100903633.title: See what happens!
summary: 'See what happens' is Motoko's default response to being told something is impossible. The results are usually explosive, occasionally brilliant, and always give Hiromi paperwork.

100998820.title: Motoko Communication interrupt
summary: Motoko's communication gets interrupted mid-call. The cutoff creates a moment of panic for whoever was on the other end, because silence from Motoko usually means violence has started.

101005696.title: Highway Star
summary: Motoko tears down a highway at speed with the intensity of Deep Purple's Highway Star. The driving sequence captures the rush of pushing a vehicle beyond its limits through Night City traffic.

101083119.title: Motoko's Parents
summary: Motoko's parents -- or rather, the memory of them -- get explored. The family she had before Night City, before the Gamer system, before everything, shapes who she is in ways chrome can't replace.

101091648.title: Death Race
summary: A Death Race scenario erupts on Night City's streets with Motoko at the center. Vehicles crash, weapons fire, and the line between racing and combat dissolves completely.

101288238.title: Motoko might have a problem
summary: Motoko might have a problem. The realization creeps in slowly as her friends start noticing patterns in her behavior that she's been ignoring. The addiction isn't to chrome -- it's to the grind.

101288766.title: A favor
summary: Someone calls in a favor from Motoko. She owes, and in Night City, debts always get collected -- usually at the worst possible time.

101293551.title: Motoko brain
summary: Motoko-brain strikes again. The specific kind of logic that makes perfect sense inside her head but looks like insanity from outside drives another sequence of events that Hiromi will have to clean up.

101334360.title: Princess is at another gang hideout.
summary: Motoko's target is at another gang hideout. The princess-rescue loop continues as she crashes yet another location to extract someone, leaving behind the usual trail of flatlined gonks.

101339889.title: A Talk Full of Hypotheticals
summary: A conversation loaded with hypotheticals lets Motoko and a contact dance around what they both know without saying it directly. In Night City, plausible deniability is a survival skill.

101350992.title: Should have gone Medic instead
summary: Someone should have gone medic instead of solo. The aftermath of a gig where combat skills weren't the bottleneck -- medical knowledge was -- drives home the cost of specialization.

101355777.title: Motoko Kusanagi was able to make this in a warehouse with an AV!
summary: Motoko builds something in a warehouse with an AV, Tony Stark style. The 'Motoko Kusanagi was able to make this in a cave with a box of scraps' energy is real.

101356779.title: Motoko/Vicky Trade Network
summary: Motoko and Vicky establish a trade network, swapping resources and intel across their respective spheres. The cross-dimensional commerce is both profitable and deeply weird.

101422890.title: For the Cosplay
summary: Motoko cosplays at a convention and her commitment to accuracy is terrifying. The costume includes functional chrome, real weapons (safety-locked), and an attitude that makes security nervous.

101424312.title: Life as a Rockstar
summary: Life as a rockstar in Night City means dodging bullets at concerts, negotiating with fixers between sets, and wondering if the audience loves you or wants to flatline you. Usually both.

101428986.title: Ghost and the Death Tank
summary: Section 9 encounters something that can only be described as a death tank. Motoko's response is to treat it as a challenge rather than a threat, because Ghost doesn't run from hardware.

101436756.title: Motoko, Cosplay Weeb.
summary: Motoko cosplays as an anime character with such dedication that people start confusing the costume for her actual combat gear. The weeb energy is indistinguishable from tactical readiness.

101442765.title: Motoko the Pirate
summary: Motoko goes pirate, commandeering a vehicle or vessel with the enthusiasm of someone who's watched too much One Piece. The crew reluctantly goes along with the nautical theming.

101443170.title: Dread Pirate Motoko
summary: Dread Pirate Motoko continues the nautical arc as she fully commits to the bit, complete with pirate terminology applied to merc operations. Hiromi files the expense report under 'maritime activities.'

101457189.title: Predator reference
summary: A Predator reference drops as Motoko faces something in the jungle-like combat zone that's hunting her back. The role reversal from predator to prey doesn't last long.

101469792.title: Diamond Dogs
summary: Diamond Dogs -- the crew takes on a gig with Metal Gear Solid V vibes, operating from a mobile base with the coordination of a PMC. Motoko slots right into the Boss role.

101477127.title: Delivery to Motoko, Merc.
summary: A delivery arrives for Motoko at her merc address. The mundane act of receiving a package becomes noteworthy because of what's inside and who sent it.

101503884.title: Dimensional Contact Established
summary: Section 9 faces xenomorph-like creatures with acid blood that nearly zeroed Adam Smasher. Malcolm moans about the damage while Hiromi asks how Motoko knew about the acid. 'I had a dream that turned into a nightmare,' Motoko says, staring into the distance.

101509533.title: Point blank
summary: Motoko realizes how far behind her team's combat skills are and buys training equipment. Point-blank drills with custom-programmed targets and spray paint help Section 9 close the gap.

101513910.title: Karada
summary: V waits for Motoko at Lizzie's Bar, tapping her fingernails impatiently while stock prices and stress alerts scroll across her vision. The kid is late and V's headache is getting worse. Their meeting about V's body -- karada -- carries weight.

101589021.title: Progress
summary: Hayato spends 793 out of 648,000 practice attempts at proper greeting protocols before meeting Motoko for sushi and dumplings. The progress is agonizingly slow but the princess pushes through.

101593872.title: Ms. Kusanagi, do enjoy your party.
summary: Wakako reads an intel report, gets to the last line, and swears. She orders Moritaka to rescind the bounty on Motoko Kusanagi immediately. Whatever the report said, it changed the fixer's calculus.

101667306.title: Pocket Sand
summary: Komorebi warns Motoko not to do something. Motoko explicitly wants to do this thing. Pocket sand gets involved. The confrontation between Tyger Claw tradition and Motoko's complete disregard for it plays out.

101668653.title: Quick Question
summary: Motoko calls Fujimura mid-gig to ask what a Shatei is. She may have just decapitated one. And threatened everyone seeking to avenge him. She just needs to know who exactly she should be worried about.

101669910.title: Payment enough
summary: Moritaka walks into his office to find three heads mounted on pikes -- Komorebi, Musashi, and Tomoe. Motoko considers the kills payment enough for the debt owed.

101676867.title: Casual Conversations
summary: Fujimura gets a terse call from Sensei: 'Daisuke has attacked Motoko.' The phone calls cascade through the Tyger Claw hierarchy as everyone scrambles to handle the fallout.

101733060.title: Just a Rainbow!
summary: Malcolm's POV on a slice-of-life moment, told using only words he would know from movies and street life. The limited vocabulary creates an earnest, unpolished perspective on the crew.

101742501.title: Motoko rant
summary: Motoko rants at Jun about disarming in front of Yakuza while Fujimura listens. Jun insists they're safe; Motoko points out the entire meeting was called specifically to hurt them. Fujimura reminds everyone he's still in the room.

101742720.title: Keep what you kill
summary: Motoko accidentally kills all the Tyger Claw leadership and the lieutenants pledge loyalty. She begs Hiromi for help: 'I don't want to run a gang!' Hiromi points out this is the second time.

101743236.title: Section 9 Delivery
summary: Motoko calls Hiromi to offer her the Tyger Claws. 'Long story short, bring Section 9 and the Minotaur.' Hiromi's first question is 'What did you do?' Motoko protests that some idiot Claw started it.

101760843.title: What The Tyger Claws Saw
summary: Kazuo watches Commander Komorebi draw his sword against the blood-stained girl. The Tyger Claws saw the Killing Flash -- Motoko's blade technique that ends fights before they begin. The rank and file witnessed something that rewrites their understanding of threat.

101832621.title: Witnesses, or take care of any witnesses
summary: Maine's crew shows up at the Tyger Claw meeting as character witnesses for Motoko. Rebecca announces they're there 'as character witnesses... or to take care of any witnesses. Whichever.'

101832855.title: Cash or card?
summary: Motoko calls Fujimura to report Yuto's corpse. He asks for the body to be sent. She mentions she's way out in Rancho Coronado. The logistics of corpse pickup across Night City districts are a pain.

101949081.title: The Flesh Disgusted me.
summary: Motoko goes in for her Gemini transplant and emerges painted on the Minotaur mech. Hiromi screams. Malcolm stifles giggles. The Mototaur was a prank all along.

101974263.title: Blessed Cube
summary: Motoko sits in a cardboard box with arm-tubes cut in it, ranting about the weakness of imperfect geometric shapes and aspiring to the purity of the Blessed Cube. Hiromi just wants to know why.

102141051.title: You Owe a Debt.
summary: Wakako calls in the debt Motoko accrued, catching her mid-street-race against Malcolm. Motoko answers her agent while drifting on the inside line, then heads to the fixer's office for a gig she can't refuse.

102371391.title: Pokemon Trainer Motoko 2: Cyber Nin-Nin Bugaloo
summary: A Pokemon crossover where Vicky Ferrous and Motoko's worlds collide. Cyber Nin-Nin Bugaloo pits Night City chrome against pocket monsters in a mashup that shouldn't work but does.

102448698.title: More things change
summary: Section 9 recognizes Motoko's different laughs and uses them to predict what she's about to do. Laugh #2 means either a diabolical plan or she thinks she's in control. Malcolm bets it's something lame she thinks is preem.

102453081.title: Breach incident report
summary: An incident report documents a minor data breach: a single netrunner tripped the secondary alarm, and Team 17 responded in 50ms. The opposing runner was already on exfil. The clinical report language masks how close the breach came.

102455289.title: Horror Soaps
summary: Motoko tracks her target to a safe house but hesitates. The mark is Jun's choom, and after experiencing TC hospitality from the target's target, she sympathizes a fraction. Horror and soap-opera drama collide.

102456495.title: Gremlin reward
summary: Fujimura asks why Yuto is standing before him alive. Motoko thought it'd be more fun to watch him get his just rewards from the Claws directly. The gremlin's idea of a reward is bringing the enemy to the executioner in person.

102456840.title: Know your value
summary: Two netrunners debate how much money would make dying to Motoko worthwhile. 'There is an amount you'll die happily for?' 'It could go to my brother.' The gallows humor of outmatched professionals.

102540501.title: What if?
summary: What-if scenarios explore how a more experienced 6th Street lieutenant could have given Motoko a harder fight with traps and better tactics. The alternate takes show how small changes cascade.

102655209.title: Displeasure
summary: Music exec Sam Henson gets a cryptic warning not to enter his office for the next few weeks. He doesn't know why -- but it's connected to the merc girl and her annoying corpo friend whose song he bought.

102773799.title: Pokemon Trainer Motoko 3: 10 years later
summary: Pokemon Trainer Motoko Part 3 jumps ten years forward. The crossover with Pokemon Trainer Vicky reaches its conclusion as both characters have grown into their respective powers.

102776793.title: The Music Industry
summary: Danny the music industry contact comes home to find a familiar teen sitting in her swiveled-around chair. Motoko has opinions about the music business and a gun to make them persuasive.

102778641.title: When you can't beat them........
summary: Motoko composes revenge music after discovering her songs were used for corporate mischief. Drums, trumpets, synth piano, maracas -- she's crafting Night City's collective suffering into a soundtrack.

102829692.title: White Tiger Gift
summary: Motoko finds a crashed Militech transport with dead guards and dead scavs. The last surviving guard hacked the robbers. The military-grade temperature-controlled box she recovers contains chrome that changes her loadout entirely.

102880593.title: the shadows that lurks
summary: Moritaka receives word that Yuto is dead and the Kamikaze assassin succeeded. An old man in Japantown lights his pipe and grumbles about wasted setups. Shadows within the Tyger Claws move.

102898365.title: Gimme Chocolate
summary: Motoko refuses to record more songs after discovering her music was licensed for Mr. Stud ads. Hiromi tries to break the loop of refusal while Motoko rants about Tank being wasted on fake dicks.

102964965.title: Heathens and SchwabSchwab
summary: Motoko heads to a Tyger Claw bar to meet Fujimura while dragging Hiromi along. The piano at the bar is fun but the info Fujimura shares changes the political landscape.

102976152.title: Tiger Balm
summary: Motoko plans Kanada Tsukimura's assassination, needing it to be obviously the work of a Tyger Claw Princess. Bullets are meh, heavy ordnance is overkill. Beheading by katana goes in the 'maybe' pile.

102979386.title: Bodyguard's View
summary: Isamu Shimazu, bodyguard to the Tyger Princess, reflects on his cushy gig. Nobody messes with Hayato, so his job is mostly breaking arms of forward teenage boys. Then he meets the Kusanagi girl.

102988110.title: 4th of July
summary: Hayato invites Motoko to a 4th of July celebration at Konpeki's penthouse. Explosive Freedom fireworks, grilled meat, and the collision of American holidays with Night City's Japanese-dominated underworld.

103093632.title: No no, this is Ykciv! Nothing to worry about!
summary: A phone call from someone warns Motoko about the Sandevistan being a Militech project, referencing supplementary materials and a Discord. Motoko has no idea who Vicky is. The cross-dimensional contact is brief and confusing.

103094196.title: The last gasp of Kanada
summary: Kanada smashes through glass, bounces off a Mr. Studd ad, and thuds down to the concrete. The rain of glass, the jazz tune, and the solid ground end his story in brutal, vivid detail.

103096002.title: Good Music
summary: Hiromi pushes Motoko to develop new advertising for her album. 'Merc rockerboy has been done to death. Has there ever been a netrunner rockerboy?' The marketing brainstorm continues.

103102539.title: Friends with (power) benefits
summary: Motoko asks Hayato for a favor, saying it's super important but only needs her by noon. The princess agrees to come immediately, proving once again she's the best friend a merc could ask for.

103126098.title: In one go
summary: Motoko tells Jun she held off on his chrome not out of anger but love. Jun argues he needs to keep up when on gigs. Motoko points at the gym downstairs and suggests eating better.

103128492.title: The TUX
summary: Motoko broke into Moritaka's house to deliver BDs his son ordered with express shipping. The tuxedo she was wearing has nothing to do with anything. Wakako is not convinced.

103130826.title: The Onryo and the Killing Flash
summary: Komorebi Izuku watches footage of Motoko's kill on Tsukimura, forcing himself to look disinterested. The Killing Flash technique earns its name as the Tyger Claw commander studies the recording frame by frame.

103200051.title: Bulk Discount
summary: Hayato's business partner asks if she had his nephew assassinated. 'Of course not,' she replies. 'I arranged for my mercenary contacts to confront him and let nature take its course.' The distinction is important to her.

103248135.title: Touch Grass journey
summary: Motoko's Touch Grass journey begins when Jun points out she hasn't moved all day. She channels Robin Williams asking 'what year is it?' and finally goes outside to find noodles and fresh air.

103325760.title: Rewards Card Confusion
summary: Motoko and Hayato meet for a gig, with Motoko still fully kitted in combat gear. Hayato looks her over, approves the impression she'll make, then starts a Rewards Card mix-up that nobody anticipated.

103330983.title: The Waiting Game
summary: Three Tyger Claws roll up on Motoko's parked Quadra with bad intentions. The Waiting Game plays out as they check their weapons and approach -- not knowing who owns the car.

103340415.title: Friends with (power) benefits 2, electric boogaloo
summary: Jun protests that Motoko keeps getting Hayato to order him to hang out with her. Motoko drags her brother to their favorite noodle shop, refusing to let go of his hand as they walk.

103343358.title: "That's really fucked Motoko."
summary: Hiromi confronts Motoko about the Onryo quickhack -- everyone she's used it on has gone cyberpsycho, killed everyone they knew, then zeroed themselves. Netwatch has been called in. Motoko insists it's not that bad.

103349433.title: True Torture.
summary: Motoko describes her new multi-stage quickhack to Hiromi: dim peripheral vision, add flickers of movement just out of sight, hijack the agent for whispers, then the real scares begin. Hiromi calls it true torture.

103378338.title: Too much Rizz
summary: Motoko's charisma exceeds safe levels. Her social skills have gotten so high that she accidentally charms people she's trying to intimidate, creating awkward situations where enemies fall in love instead of running.

103428594.title: Berserker
summary: Motoko swallows her pride and asks Jun for something -- the trigger for a berserker-mode project she's been working on. The vulnerability of admitting she needs help from her brother drives the scene.

103430934.title: (No!) Quests.
summary: Motoko's Gamer system switches from XP-on-kill to a quest system. 'Quest Unlocked: Night City's Biggest Gremlin -- Successfully prank Adam Smasher.' Motoko stares at the notification in dawning horror.

103547232.title: Slow Elevator Ride
summary: Regina and Kiwi share a tense elevator ride after whatever just happened. Kiwi presses about the Kang Tao blockade. Regina is too mentally exhausted to discuss it. The slow elevator makes the silence worse.

103651971.title: Just according to Keikaku. (Keikaku means plan.)
summary: Motoko hangs up on Hiromi, who's been buried in contract sub-clauses for a week. Motoko's 'great idea' to speed things up involves Kuroiwa, a record label exec, and methods Hiromi definitely wouldn't approve of. Just according to keikaku.

103658139.title: Bestest Assassin (Infiltrator) EVAR!
summary: Hayato has a bad week -- stubbed toes, falling glasses, a missing hairpin. Everything points to someone testing her defenses subtly. The bestest assassin infiltrator ever is running reconnaissance on the Tyger Princess.

103663932.title: Hot Spring Bonus
summary: Hayato gets Motoko's team a reserved hot spring as a bonus for handling Tsukimura. The slice-of-life reward for a job well done lets Section 9 decompress in style.

103681704.title: Malware Taste
summary: Motoko discovers cooking is a skill when she gets a level-up notification making curry. She immediately rejects it -- too isekai protagonist, too generic. Then Sasha calls and she realizes even food is weaponizable.

104725669.title: DON'T LET THE GREMLINS TALK!
