summary: Junior Professor Vicky Ferrous struggles with her paper Pokedex years after the PTV crossover. The Gremlins -- Motoko's influence -- have left their mark on the Pokemon world. Don't let them talk.

105874418.title: Cyber Gotham
summary: Motoko finds an old ad referencing Gotham City and her brain short-circuits. The existence of Batman's city in the Cyberpunk universe opens a rabbit hole Jun immediately regrets witnessing.

105996443.title: Training Day
summary: A Swiss Sovereign Wealth Fund general manager's quiet morning gets disrupted by his new neighbor -- some rich kid whose 'training day' involves enough noise and chaos to rival a small war.

106352429.title: Jun (Almost) get's lucky.
summary: Jun almost gets lucky with a woman who peels off her jacket and pushes him back -- until the moment shatters. Something interrupts. It always interrupts. The Oni's love life remains cursed.

107520650.title: Follow the Ghost
summary: Tachikoma follows Creator's implicit directive to learn new things. Standing, walking, The Edge of The Table, falling, pushing, climbing -- each lesson builds toward something the little bot doesn't yet understand.

107934116.title: I need a hero!
summary: Motoko calls Jun for help from inside a closet where someone blocked the door while she was hacking a scav server. The girl who solos borg squads needs her brother to let her out of a supply room.

108475931.title: Do it all again
summary: V needs netrunning help that doesn't involve Arasaka. She swallows her pride and calls Motoko, whose young voice on the phone reminds V she's asking a kid for help with deeply personal chrome work.

108650825.title: Orphys First Gig
summary: Orphy's first gig at a bar with truly awful acoustics. The bad sound does something wonderful to the music -- it strips away production and leaves raw feeling. The audience hears what could be.

108668015.title: A SCIENTIFIC blunder
summary: A Fallout crossover where someone both smart and dumb enough to create the Gamer system chip lost it. The SCIENTIFIC blunder traces back to a vault where Six wakes up to arguing about the missing prototype.

108680234.title: Code name Phil
summary: Motoko builds her iconic weapon -- Code Name Phil. What starts as maintenance day becomes a creation session as she forges something that, when seen or heard, makes Night City take notice.

108696896.title: Posession question
summary: Sasha visits fixer Mac in Watson, unsure of her welcome. The man is laid back but connected enough that pissing him off ruins your rep across the city. The meeting sets up a major new job.

108868211.title: A talk with a Tiger Mom
summary: The Fallout crossover continues as Wakako confronts Six with the curt tone of an old acquaintance. 'You're alive, are you?' The reunion between the wasteland wanderer and the Night City fixer spans years of history.

109122713.title: Blank Slate
summary: A man enters a dark apartment, sets down groceries, lights incense at a shrine with two pictures, and cooks a traditional meal correctly. The blank slate of someone starting over, defined only by what they choose to remember.

109127846.title: Make them remember
summary: Alice's perspective during a freak-out, her thoughts doubling and racing as the Violent Hemorrhage singer processes something that rocks her world. The writing breaks rules to match her panicked mental state.

109138403.title: No love songs
summary: Motoko flops in bed with too much energy, complaining it's too early while Jun yells it's near 1 PM. She writes music that isn't love songs, explicitly, even though Night City keeps trying to make her feel things.

109259537.title: Ion Frontier Family: Review
summary: Scott Marcay, Ion Frontier Seattle's purchasing team leader, faces an All Manager Confab on a Tuesday morning. The corporate meeting reveals supply chain disruptions connected to Motoko's exploits.

109266683.title: A Security Professional
summary: Mac gets called in for net security consulting -- his rarer gig compared to his usual data theft work. He walks into the building with his briefcase and charming smile, ready to assess whatever disaster hit their servers.

109282391.title: X-Treme BRAIN-DANCE REVIEW
summary: Motoko reviews her own X-Treme Braindance of a knife throw that missed. 'Fuckity fuck fuck fuck. I couldn't exactly GO BACK AND DO THIS SHIT AGAIN!' The flub is immortalized in the XBD for everyone to see.

109299935.title: Failure to diversify
summary: Failure to diversify catches up with someone. The financial lesson -- applied to combat, assets, or relationships -- costs more than eddies when the single point of failure breaks.

109340561.title: Standard Graft Policy
summary: Standard graft policy in Night City's corporate world gets examined. The corruption is so normalized it has procedures, forms, and approved vendors.

109498403.title: What was this for again?
summary: Someone asks 'what was this for again?' midway through a gig that's spiraled so far from the original objective that nobody remembers why they started.

109521242.title: Another day in Night City.
summary: Another day in Night City. The routine violence, the casual chrome, the noodle shops, and the body counts that make the morning news. Normal by local standards.

109600316.title: Murderer sitting next to you
summary: The realization that a murderer is sitting next to you at the bar. The casual coexistence of killers and civilians in Night City spaces, where the person buying you a drink might have flatlined someone an hour ago.

109956152.title: Militech BG-80 toastmaster
summary: A Militech BG-80 toastmaster -- some obscure piece of military hardware -- gets examined, discussed, or deployed. The specific model number suggests someone did their homework on Cyberpunk's weapon catalog.

110054309.title: Smasher Raid
summary: Smasher raids a location. When Adam Smasher personally shows up to wreck something, the scale of destruction is measured in structural damage rather than body count.

110156585.title: Motoko plays her own boss music.
summary: Motoko plays her own boss music during a fight. The speakers blast her combat soundtrack while she wades into enemies, turning every engagement into a concert nobody bought tickets for.
A short piece tied to chapter 76 of the main story. The number '76' references both the chapter and the Cyberpunk 2076 timeline.

110232308.title: PUBLIC SAFETY ANNOUNCEMENT
summary: A PUBLIC SAFETY ANNOUNCEMENT goes out -- presumably warning Night City citizens about Motoko's latest activity, framed in the bureaucratic language of a city that can't control its most dangerous resident.

110234360.title: Bored now
summary: Motoko's 'bored now' is the most dangerous phrase in Night City. When the grind stalls and the gigs dry up, a bored Motoko finds entertainment in ways that make fixers nervous.

110234531.title: BD Review: Onryo
summary: A BD review of the Onryo -- Motoko's ghost persona -- analyzed frame by frame by enthusiasts who don't realize they're reviewing footage of a real person's lethal operations.

110238440.title: And she's...
summary: 'And she's...' -- the trailing realization as someone figures out who Motoko actually is. The moment of recognition when civilian meets legend.

110238629.title: Through Chaos, Profit
summary: Through chaos, profit. Hiromi's motto applied to whatever disaster Motoko just created. Every explosion has a silver lining if your fixer knows where to invoice.

110240333.title: Trunk Tachikoma
summary: A Tachikoma stows away in a car trunk. The spider-bot's adventure in confined spaces results in exactly the kind of chaos a curious AI with legs would create.

110240906.title: Wrong Business
summary: Someone walks into the wrong business at the wrong time. The mix-up between legitimate commerce and merc operations creates a fish-out-of-water moment in Night City.

110271350.title: The offices of Logan, Anderson & Wick
summary: The offices of Logan, Anderson & Wick -- a law firm built on three legendary solos going legit. The name L.A.W. is both the acronym and the joke.

110279231.title: Offices of Logan Anderson & Wick Pt 2
summary: L.A.W. Part 2 continues the adventures of the solo law firm. The senior partners navigate corpo legal battles with the same intensity they used to bring to firefights.

110282516.title: Court of L.A.W. Is a bit confusing.
summary: The Court of L.A.W. proceedings get confusing as legal terminology mixes with merc slang. Nobody in the courtroom is sure which rules apply -- law or street.

110310242.title: 'Stolen' Car
summary: A stolen car creates complications. In Night City, vehicle theft is practically a sport, but this particular car belongs to the wrong person.

110358515.title: Ad Space
summary: Ad space in Night City gets weaponized -- someone is using holographic billboards, news tickers, or building-side screens for purposes the advertisers definitely didn't approve.

110360030.title: Malcolm's side Gig
summary: Malcolm takes a side gig that has nothing to do with Section 9. The solo outing tests whether Malcolm can handle biz on his own without Motoko's overwhelming presence.

110370275.title: Ghost 'instinct'
summary: Motoko's ghost instinct fires -- the sixth sense that warns her before trouble arrives. She's already moving before the conscious mind catches up.

110374208.title: Delta-V
summary: Maximum Mike's morning show kicks off against the backdrop of 49 daily deaths, gang shootings in Northside and Kabuki, and morgues burning day and night. Delta-V captures the velocity of Night City's body count.

110401184.title: The offices of Logan, Anderson & Wick Pt 3
summary: The three senior partners of L.A.W., plus Hiromi and Motoko, sit at a conference table with CrystalDome windows. The solo law firm's Part 3 gets properly serious about their legal-meets-merc operations.

110496440.title: Barghest One month Later
summary: One month after joining Barghest, Motoko picks up sketchy chrome off the back of a Villefort. Hiromi rolls her eyes at the cliche delivery method until something catches her eye and changes everything.

110848109.title: Parry: Explanation BD
summary: A Braindance tutorial on parrying starts with the sound of metal touching metal. Bold red letters: 'Explanation.' Motoko's quiet voice walks through blade techniques while examining equipment. Recorded as a charity for the NCPD.

110853005.title: Parry a bullet, parry a Quickhack
summary: Motoko's logic chain: if she can parry a bullet, she can parry a sword. If she can parry a bullet, why not a quickhack? Sasha sputters that parrying bullets shouldn't work in the first place.

110869469.title: Wrong definition
summary: Hiromi catches Motoko 'hacking' scav computers by chopping them with her sword. 'Still getting hacking experience,' Motoko insists. Hiromi does not think that word means what Motoko thinks it means.

110921099.title: Older Man Thing
summary: Jun confronts Motoko about her 'older man thing.' Dr. Vik as a ripper is fine, Tino helped her -- but Sensei? Motoko's innocent confusion suggests Jun is reading way too much into her mentor relationships.

110961653.title: Mokoto: The Inner Weeb Awakens
summary: Jun comes home to find Motoko has gone full weeb. Dark outfit, anime quotes, dramatic posing. The Inner Weeb Awakens and Jun has seen enough anime-brain Motoko to know this phase will be insufferable.

110963162.title: Consequences of Being a Gonk
summary: Vik stitches up Motoko and asks how she got a split bullet in her gut. 'Training accident,' she offers, but it sounds like a question. Vik orders her to elaborate. The Consequences of Being a Gonk catch up.

110963618.title: Memories Broken
summary: Motoko sings 'Memories Broken' from Metal Gear Rising, replacing key lyrics with 'EX-P.' The only thing she knows for real is that there will be XP. The Metal Gear reference slides naturally into her grind mentality.

111048266.title: Hella Petty
summary: Motoko demands they bomb Arasaka Tower because they're holding the 8K UltraHD of the latest anime season hostage. Hiromi mentions her family has access. 'ANIME YEARNS TO BE FREE!' Motoko rants. Jun asks Hiromi why his sister is yelling at nothing.

111081791.title: The body is weak
summary: Rita finds a ninja obstacle course on Lizzies' roof with Judy training on it. Susie explains: Motoko's braindances gave Judy body dysmorphia issues, and now she's doing acrobatics in borrowed Mox gym clothes.

111084224.title: Tony's Input
summary: Melissa watches a BD snippet of Tony's death on loop. She doesn't care about the scavs dying -- Tony was an idiot who stole the wrong car. But the precision of the kill tells her something about who did it.

111085751.title: Take care of the fish, or else
summary: A screen displays a plea to Ghost not to kill someone because nobody would feed his fish. The response drips with disdain: 'Fish are made for cats to eat.' Then comes the Cool -- the professional assessment of whether the request has value.

111094814.title: Ripperdoc Motoko
summary: Ichi watches Motoko become a teenage ripperdoc and tries to ignore the sights and sounds. Her gremlin tendencies plus medical chrome equals a healthcare experience nobody would voluntarily sign up for.

111105758.title: Meet the Motokos
summary: Meet the Motokos -- TF2 style. Motoko walks up to camera, places her HMG on a cardboard box, and introduces herself as Heavy Weapons Motoko. Her weapon weighs 128 pounds, fires two hundred dollar custom-tooled cartridges, and costs 400,000 eddies to fire for twelve seconds.

111109118.title: Section 9, meet the driver titlecard
summary: Meet the Driver title card: Ichi flicks a scav-masked bobblehead off the Behemoth's dash. 'Boom, get fucked.' The TF2-style intro captures Ichi's dry humor as distant gunfire provides ambiance.

111123086.title: CyberPsycho Song
summary: A CyberPsycho song written for Motoko's band -- mad flies buzzing, roaches running, voices in your head, pull off your hand, load up your arm, time to make them dead. BOOM.

111125012.title: Meet the Rebecca:
summary: Meet the Rebecca -- TF2 style. Rebecca narrowly escapes an explosion while jumping from a Night City corridor. 'What makes me a good merc? If I were a bad merc, I wouldn't be sitting here.'

111132053.title: Meet the... Burrito
summary: Motoko comes home to an empty apartment, her day of netrunning and scav-hunting over. She calls for Jun, gets no answer, and discovers a situation involving a burrito that shouldn't be sentient but technically is.

111134588.title: THE ONRYO IS IN THE BASE!
summary: Brick sprints toward the netrunner den as the alert blares: THE ONRYO IS IN THE BASE. He shouts to wake sleeping Maelstrom gangers while hoping the Ghost hasn't already reached the basement. The TF2-meets-Cyberpunk raid plays out in real time.

111135743.title: Meet the Corpo
summary: Meet the Corpo -- Hiromi sits in a dimmed office with acoustic Motoko covers playing. 'I solve practical problems. Not conundrums of philosophy. I solve the problem of how to keep some idiot accounting gonk from ripping us off.'

111139295.title: Meet Tachikoma.
summary: Meet Tachikoma -- the spider-bot walks on screen with a flamethrower, stepping on torn-out optics while Happy Farm plays from its hull speakers. A borg's silhouette in the background admits it's afraid.

111222578.title: Delivery boy
summary: Mac interviews David Martinez for a delivery job. Top of his class at Arasaka Academy, never sold black lace (just XBDs like a creepy gonk). Hiromi taught him to be a better salesman.

111274904.title: No Scavs, Free Weapons
summary: Motoko writes NSFW on scav den walls after hits. Hiromi objects. Motoko explains it means 'No Scavs, Free Weapons.' Hiromi points out nobody interprets NSFW that way.

111363272.title: Too Much Butterflies
summary: Too many butterflies -- a cyberpsycho attack on Arasaka Tower kills Hiromi's parents among the casualties. Section 9 hunts for the psycho and Motoko discovers the culprit is someone she knows.

111398549.title: Don't klep my Car backfire
summary: Band members learn Motoko's preem Quadra was stolen. They panic about being associated with a klepped car and try to disavow her. Night City vehicle theft carries fatal consequences if the owner is connected.

111402284.title: not paying attention
summary: Motoko clarifies she killed the car's owner and everyone he knew first, then had it repainted and modded. Her bandmates relax immediately. Meanwhile, Raffen in the Badlands find BD footage of their stolen car in NC.

111432611.title: David Martinez best day ever.
summary: David Martinez stalks outside a restaurant, finally done watching Ghost's BDs for the hundredth time. His mother caught him drooling with a BD wreath after school. Today he takes the first step toward being a real merc.

111438938.title: Davids Rules
summary: David gets read the rules: never cheap out on equipment, always maintain software, and when it's time for chrome, Gloria's ripper only. The glare that enforces these rules is fiercer than any chrome.

111546686.title: The C----erunner
summary: David joins Section 9 months after joining up, standing straight-backed with a grunt's confidence. Hiromi looks at the trained operative and sees the result of Motoko's influence turning a student into a runner.

111602654.title: Koma Media Empire
summary: Motoko's advances in Tachikoma AI lead to the Song Bird Implant -- a voice modulation device that lets her maintain her Sexy Major voice. A Koma Media Empire is born as the tech goes commercial.

111606542.title: Shy Shy
summary: Rebecca sells Hiromi intel about Motoko for eddies. The info involves something that makes Hiromi's eyes go wide -- something about her 'one and only' that changes the dynamic between the two.

111763085.title: Kya!
summary: Motoko squeals like a Japanese romcom character and immediately denies it. Rebecca cackles. Kiwi confirms the squeal was exact. Motoko covers her blushing face while insisting 'fuck you.'

111763361.title: Sasha The Nyanrunner
summary: Sasha feels the data moat slam shut and panics for Motoko's safety. Daemons spawn and Sasha uploads ICE while firing offensive quickhacks. The Nyanrunner fights to protect her partner in the net.

111763892.title: Incident Report 12-2-44-77
summary: Johanna Vess disconnects from her netrunning chair to find Mo Reese slumped at his desk, dripping water on his keyboard. The incident report 12-2-44-77 covers whatever just happened in the net.

111776456.title: David and Lucy's first meeting
summary: David tells Motoko about his life over beer (that his mother can never find out about). Their first meeting happened after Motoko won over Gloria despite a rocky start. David feels like he's finally going somewhere.

111805748.title: Truman Technical solutions
summary: Truman Technical Solutions' Wallace delivers bad news: the hardware is slagged and all server data should be considered compromised. The skill level of the attacker puts this in a different threat category.

111922775.title: Johnny Bravo
summary: After describing Adam Smasher's Gemini body, people point out he looks like Johnny Bravo. Project Cynosure and the Canto Mk 6 cyberdeck get discussed alongside the visual comparison.

112000202.title: A not so violent bar-escapade
summary: Violent Hemorrhage band members sit in a bar wondering how they ended up following a teenage girl as their leader. Klein asks when they'll be famous. The answer involves more of Motoko's chaos than they expected.

112224362.title: Carnage Girl
summary: Tori gets dragged to a club by Dylan and witnesses Motoko's concert. The experience transforms an ordinary night into something that cracks open her understanding of what music can do in Night City.

112224827.title: 2nd Carnage Girl
summary: Satsuriki Otome, the carnage shotgun girl, feels the bass pulse into her as Motoko's concert hits full intensity. The music changes something inside her. The fan's POV captures what it's like to be consumed by the performance.

112225775.title: She's here again
summary: Rebecca nudges Motoko backstage. 'She's here again.' The shotgun girl -- always front row, always with the Carnage. Motoko fumes at the superfan's persistence. Rebecca finds it hilarious.

112232087.title: Motoko Songs by Genre. (up to Chapter 229)
summary: A categorized list of every song Motoko has performed through Chapter 229 -- Argent Metal, Bebop Jazz, and more. The catalog shows the breadth of her musical range across genres.

112240286.title: Another Face in the Audience
summary: Another face in the audience watches Motoko's concert after multiple re-reads of the chapter. The POV of a random club-goer who came to get wasted and was pleasantly destroyed by the music instead.

112250003.title: Malcolm: Cougar Hunter
summary: Malcolm draws unwanted attention from older women. Hiromi sprouts metaphorical cat ears in cuteness while a can of Tiancha bounces off a grandpa's head. Dino holds up a 10/10 scorecard for the throw.

112447598.title: Rip and tear. Until it is done
summary: Robert Wilson comes out of a BD scrolling session with the words 'Rip and tear, until it is done' echoing. The DOOM-inspired omake follows a merc inspired by Motoko's combat BDs to go full Slayer.

112556078.title: Vehicle Log entry: #4823
summary: Vehicle Log entry #4823: Motoko fiddles with a nabbed shard after a gig, curiosity getting the better of professionalism. Whatever Dino's scheme actually was, the shard data reveals more than expected.

113083451.title: Fight like Hell
summary: Motoko works on a DOOM-inspired song with lyrics -- 'Fight Like Hell.' The beat is wrong at first, too synthetic, too smooth. She tears it apart and rebuilds until it sounds like an actual soundtrack to violence.

113799596.title: David The Cringewalker
summary: David falls to the cringe side, becoming the Cringewalker -- his path of maximum embarrassment spiraling as he tries to be cool and fails in increasingly spectacular fashion.

113820896.title: Drill Instructor
summary: Motoko hires a drill instructor on the cheap without telling Hiromi. The intro goes sideways when Hiromi demands to meet this highly skilled and expensive professional -- only to find someone unexpected.

113823578.title: Johnny Silverhand wakes up as a Tachikoma
summary: Johnny Silverhand wakes up in a Tachikoma body. Audio online, optics online, limbs online -- and then he starts poking around his new spider-bot chassis. His reaction to being a mechanical arachnid is exactly what you'd expect from Johnny.

113828051.title: Born in Night City, by Stand Alone Complex:
summary: A Night City rendition of 'Born in the USA' -- 'Born in Night City' by Stand Alone Complex. The protest song's satirical bite translates perfectly to the Cyberpunk setting's social commentary.

113835425.title: Cyberpunk Motoko: Phantom Lunchbreak.
summary: The events of Phantom Liberty become anti-climactic as Motoko sits in a cafe with Hiromi, sipping dubious chemicals while Hiromi enjoys real Brazil coffee. The DLC's spy thriller plots fold quickly when Motoko is involved.

114012197.title: I Can't Believe It's Not Idris Elba
summary: Motoko sings along with herself on the radio, eyes flashing yellow with each call. V rings up and Motoko answers: 'I-Am-In-Your-Walls Incorporated, how can I help you?' 'Fuck off, kid.' The banter flows.

114019790.title: On the list
summary: Mac walks into a building wearing an actual suit and finger guns. 'How the fuck did you even get in here?' 'My name's on the guest list.' Jago checks the tablet and finds it's actually true.

114066611.title: Unique Hacks
summary: Mac teaches Sasha and Motoko about custom quickhack programs. Maelstrom gangers dance uncontrollably to old music while Motoko tries not to laugh. 'The real fun is in custom programs,' Mac grins.

114110579.title: Worm Ramen - Smasher vs Motoko fight
summary: Adam Smasher and Motoko face off before their long-awaited fight. Smasher reviews her file in a millisecond -- impressed and cautious. The staredown before the clash crackles with lethal potential.

114132854.title: Gun Dispenser Motoko
summary: Motoko doesn't want to do another club gig with the band. She's doing it for eddies and catharsis only. That's her story and she's sticking to it, even as Gun Dispenser Motoko hands out weapons to fans mid-set.

114360329.title: Double Motoko
summary: Two Motokos coexist -- Arasaka Motoko and Kusanagi Motoko. Hiromi assumes K!Motoko is a fan of the other Motoko. K!Motoko violently disagrees. The identity confusion makes life harder for both.

114346436.title: The garden, as everything, had two faces.
summary: Saburo Arasaka rests on the engawa of his garden, eyes closed, letting nostalgia fill his senses -- flowers, cut grass, warm stones. The garden has two faces, and beneath the beauty lies everything that built the Arasaka empire.

114355196.title: Are two Motokos better then one?
summary: The doorbell interrupts Motoko's weapon calibration. A second Motoko stands in the doorway. 'Are two Motokos better than one?' explores the chaos of identity duplication in Night City.

114836944.title: Brother Of The Fist
summary: Jun sits with bags under his eyes in a side room at the Dojo, asking Sensei for guidance. The conversation with his mentor touches on duty, family, and the weight of being the Oni while watching his sister become something beyond human.

114856255.title: Claim Jumped
summary: Mac's crew comes back from a Militech heist looking defeated -- they got in clean but the items were already gone. Someone claim-jumped them. Mac types furiously, trying to figure out who beat them to the punch.

115221178.title: Drive better drunk
summary: Hiromi and Motoko sit in a club for a gig that requires just the two of them in person. Getting their target to talk requires Hiromi's experience applied directly. The drive home involves someone driving better drunk than sober.

115400425.title: "Shotgun girls" Fury Road
summary: Motoko and Section 9 are pinned by forty-plus scavs when 189 shotgun girls show up as reinforcements. Hiromi's reaction to learning about the armed fan club is something Motoko would rather delay.

115920352.title: Evil Sound Tech
summary: The Evil Sound Tech adjusts the reverb. Motoko carefully sets down her guitar, storms over, returns the control to its previous value, and makes a threat about throat-slitting that nobody doubts she means.

115920850.title: Wage Slave
summary: Motoko's studio day goes better than expected. The sound tech is a mousy exotic girl with actual mouse ears, and the professionals have less ego than anticipated. Wage slave life isn't all bad.

115922089.title: Reverb slider
summary: A newbie sound tech notices tape over the reverb control and asks about it. His coworker goes pale, grabs him in a headlock-huddle, and hisses 'SSSSHH!' The reverb slider has a history no one wants to repeat.

115922821.title: Shotguns girls live.
summary: Net forum debate about shotgun girls: i_XBD_Lover_420 insists they're fake and staged. CheeseMan points out the recordings are live. Only 12 out of 180 died during initiation. The internet argues about Motoko's armed fanbase.

115929019.title: Rockerboy Overwatch
summary: Motoko provides rockerboy overwatch at a concert -- three walls of ICE down, two to go, routing attacks through a fast food joint across the street while performing on stage. Multitasking at its most extreme.

116059051.title: Airforce Cadet Motoko 1 Stargate SG-1 Cross.
summary: Motoko jumps into a random portal and ends up at Stargate Command. Air Force Cadet Motoko meets SG-1 in a 3,800-word crossover that explores what happens when Night City's ghost meets the USAF.

116060506.title: Bt dubs
summary: Motoko kicks her feet on the exam table asking how far along they are in the 'real life Star Gate SG-1 scenario.' The finger-curling rapid-fire questions barrel through plot logistics at merc speed.

116060986.title: Doctor Reports
summary: Dr. Fraiser reports to General Hammond about their guest: a full-body cyborg with technology beyond anything SGC has encountered. The medical assessment of Motoko through a Stargate lens reveals how alien her chrome is to a military doctor.

116075560.title: Wake up Samurai, We got a Galaxy to Burn
summary: Daniel Jackson bribes Motoko with real ramen. She hasn't had real noodles in ages and devours the bowl. 'Wait, there are no noodles on your earth?' The cultural exchange between Night City and Stargate Command continues over food.

117877010.title: My Car!
summary: Adam Smasher planned a superhero landing on Motoko's car to crush her escape. Next thing he knew, he was flying in the wrong direction. Nobody touches Motoko's ride.

118092293.title: The Burrito
summary: Jun startles awake to Motoko offering breakfast to him and his 'friend.' Jun is confused -- nobody else is there. He looks around and realizes Motoko found the burrito he fell asleep cuddling.

118111388.title: 100% Vickytory!
summary: Hayato calls with a gig: someone embarrassed her family and needs to be flatlined with humiliation. Motoko was expecting a sparring session but a gig works too. 100% Vickytory is the mission codename.

118139354.title: Forgot the eddies
summary: Motoko reflects on her growing following of shotgun girls who attend every show. One of them reached Hiromi, who passed along a message. Motoko forgot to collect the eddies from the last gig in the excitement.

118283660.title: Jun almost gets lucky 2
summary: Jun tries again with Rita after running out on her last time. 'Give me one reason after you tossed me,' Rita glares. Jun struggles to explain that it was about Motoko, not her. The Oni's love life attempt two.

118318253.title: Straight for the Castle
summary: Motoko heads to stage with Hiromi asking if she's sure. 'About this? Yeah. About the consequences? Fuck no.' The wedding is in the morning and this is the last warning before going nuclear. She storms straight for the castle.

118318898.title: Onyro and Oni song (Unintentionally)
summary: Motoko unknowingly writes a theme song for herself and Jun -- the Onryo and the Oni. Working late with her laptop's glow the only light, she layers sirens, AV rumble, and bar noise into a track that captures their shared legend.

118338212.title: Tachikoma's big day
summary: Tachikoma's big day as told by HMG-chan (the gun), who thinks of herself as big sister. The gun narrates Tachikoma's stumbling development with cheerleader enthusiasm as the spider-bot falls off tables and walks into walls.

118369727.title: I just want to groove baby yeah!
summary: Motoko has been running around for hours handling everyone's emergencies. Finally free, she just wants noodles and to groove. Hiromi is the only person who didn't need something from her today.

118423127.title: My funny Valentine
summary: Jun asks Motoko to push their Valentine's Day lunch, clearly trying to ditch her for a date. Motoko gives him grief for being suspicious but ultimately lets him go, because her brother deserves happiness.

119123276.title: Due Diligence
summary: The record company hasn't done due diligence on Motoko yet. When they finally background-check their new talent and discover the teenage musician is also a hardcore merc with a triple-digit body count, the corporate reaction will be special.

119144789.title: MSM Agent
summary: An MSM agent wraps up a phone call with Hiromi, asking if Motoko can come to the studio tomorrow. The mundane scheduling masks the corporate machinery grinding around Night City's most dangerous pop star.

119212733.title: Don't Fuck with your Grunts
summary: Mikey rips off his BD wreath, coughing and shaking after experiencing something that made him mutter 'what the fuck' on repeat. The combat BD he just viewed was someone's real gig -- and it terrified a professional.

119265839.title: Fish in a Pond
summary: Rogue gets a call from an old contact while accepting gigs at the Afterlife. The Fish in a Pond -- Motoko's presence in Night City's merc ecosystem has grown large enough that even the Queen of the Afterlife is recalculating.

119268569.title: The File and The Clause
summary: Motoko asks Alex two questions: what are the top 10 facts in MSM's file on her, and what's in the clause she definitely should have read. The File and The Clause reveal how much the corporation knows.

119272886.title: Never do an enemy a small injury.
summary: 'Never do an enemy a small injury' -- Motoko quotes Machiavelli to Hiromi. Go after their ability to hurt you, not their pride. Stop them from being an enemy at all. Hiromi agrees it's good advice but finds petty revenge more fun.

119280455.title: How was the party?
summary: Kitty tells someone about a cute singer she met at a party -- Motoko Ku-something. The moment her companion recognizes the full name, the tone shifts. Everyone in Night City knows Motoko Kusanagi except the people who just met her.

119951724.title: Out with the old
summary: Motoko asks the MSM manager if he's checking out her new arms. They don't look new, he says. 'I've got a guy. He supplied the arms.' 'A guy?' 'Your guy.' The revelation that Motoko got her chrome from the manager's own supplier.

120343878.title: Tracking Motoko
summary: Alex sends a team to shadow Motoko after she leaves the studio. She drove around, found a gang fight pit, beat the champion, started a gang fight, and killed about a dozen bangers in close combat. Routine stress relief.

120345984.title: Need to relax
summary: Alex considers sending Motoko on tour overseas. If she likes violence so much, maybe she'd find success abroad. A before-and-after Militech brand deal fight is suggested. The corpo scrambles to manage their most dangerous talent.

120349605.title: Surfin' U.S.A.
summary: Motoko ducks haymakers while headlocking a 6th Street ganger on a rooftop when NCPD lights up the sky. 'Cheese it! It's the fuzz!' Everyone scrambles as she sprints to the roof edge and surfs down, Surfin' USA style.

120349761.title: Penny drop
summary: Alex walks into a meeting room and finds a BD chair with a high-end wreath waiting. 'Before we begin, there is something you need to see,' Hiromi says. The penny finally drops as the MSM exec experiences Motoko's combat firsthand.

120361512.title: That Time Judy was Wrong
summary: Judy greets Motoko with 'Fuck you' because the latest BD has her riding a guy off a building and Judy doesn't like heights. Motoko claims she gave a warning -- and has BD evidence of Judy receiving it. That time Judy was wrong.

120372126.title: A new gig
summary: Motoko de-stresses at the range after dealing with MSM and the jerk who tried to replace Hiromi. A calculation runs in the back of her brain: the jerk hasn't reached the kill threshold yet, but he's cruising toward it. A new gig notification pings.

120375429.title: One's a job, the other's mental illness
summary: Section 9 interrogates a Maelstrom ganger while debating whether Motoko is a serial killer, spree killer, or assassin. Hiromi argues spree killer based on activity levels. Malcolm declares she's both. The ganger offers to call her 'perfectly healthy by teenage standards' if they let him go. They don't.

120376428.title: Then I will just sing to the stars!
summary: Motoko storms away from soul-crushing cookie-cutter MSM performances. Voices chase her but she keeps walking. If the corporation won't let her play real music, she'll just sing to the stars -- and she does, climbing to a rooftop and performing for no one but the Night City skyline.

120379470.title: Test Run
summary: Motoko tests her new arms' fine motor control by grabbing Alex's balls in a vice grip. 'That was a bitch move trying to oust Hiromi,' she explains calmly. The test run is both functional and punitive.

120441495.title: Birthday Bash
summary: Fujimura arrives at Motoko's surprise birthday party and immediately asks Jun if she accidentally shot, maimed, or exploded someone when she found out. Jun assures him everyone is in one piece. So far.

120441510.title: Clearly Marked Pit of Doom
summary: Jacob stares at a gaping pit in the middle of MSM's 39th floor hallway, surrounded by signs reading 'WARNING! PIT OF DOOM! DO NOT ENTER!' and 'PLEASE GO AROUND CAREFULLY!' The pit is still there when he blinks.

120448965.title: No one steals my car, electric boogalo
summary: Nobody steals Motoko's car -- electric boogaloo. The second iteration of someone learning the hard way that touching Motoko's ride has consequences that escalate exponentially.

120514914.title: The Motoko Scale
summary: A mid-level merc watches Motoko clear a room and whispers 'what a badass.' The Motoko Scale is born -- a measurement system where other mercs rate combat scenarios by how many Motokos it would take to solve them. One is always enough.
